  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the adjustment was automatically generated by a system process (e.g., Blue Yonder replenishment system, RFID reconciliation engine, WMS cycle count automation) rather than manually entered by a user.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory node type where the adjustment occurred. Distinguishes between store-level adjustments, Distribution Center (DC) adjustments, Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC) adjustments, and dark store adjustments for segmented shrinkage and variance reporting.']
  lp_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this inventory adjustment to an active or closed Loss Prevention (LP) investigation case (e.g., shoplifting incident, employee theft case). Nullable — only populated when the adjustment is associated with a formal LP case in Salesforce Service Cloud or a dedicated LP system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or explanations provided by the initiating or authorizing user regarding the inventory adjustment. Supports audit trail completeness and loss prevention investigation documentation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the inventory adjustment is posted to the stock ledger and general ledger. May differ from adjustment_timestamp when adjustments are batched or backdated for period-end closing. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  quantity_after_adjustment decimal(15,4) [note: 'The on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location immediately after this adjustment is applied. Derived at posting time and stored for audit trail and stock ledger reconciliation. Expressed in the SKU\'s unit of measure.']
  quantity_before_adjustment decimal(15,4) [note: 'The on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location immediately prior to this adjustment being applied. Captured at the time of adjustment for audit trail completeness and variance analysis. Expressed in the SKU\'s unit of measure.']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of why the inventory adjustment is being made. Drives reporting buckets for shrinkage monitoring, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) impact analysis, and loss prevention dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shrinkage|damage|cycle_count_correction|receiving_variance|expiry_write_off|rtv|admin_correction|found_stock — promote to reference product]']
  reason_sub_category varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the reason category providing specific cause detail. Examples for shrinkage: shoplifting, employee theft, administrative error, vendor fraud. Examples for damage: in-transit damage, handling damage, shelf damage. Feeds loss prevention case analysis and shrinkage root-cause reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shoplifting|employee_theft|admin_error|vendor_fraud|in_transit_damage|handling_damage|shelf_damage|expired|recalled|mis-ship — promote to reference product]']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this inventory adjustment is associated with a product recall event initiated by the CPSC, FDA, or the retailer. Enables rapid identification and financial quantification of recall-related inventory write-offs.']
  rfid_tag_count varchar [note: 'Number of RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tags scanned during the detection event that triggered this adjustment. Populated only for RFID-based detection methods. Supports RFID program accuracy measurement and tag read-rate analysis.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the originating source document (e.g., cycle count ID, RTV authorization number, receiving document number, damage report ID). Enables cross-system traceability and audit trail linkage.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of source business document that triggered or supports this inventory adjustment. Provides traceability back to the originating business process (e.g., cycle count sheet, Return to Vendor (RTV) authorization, receiving document). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cycle_count|physical_inventory|rtv_authorization|receiving_document|damage_report|expiry_report|pos_exception_report — promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit at the time of adjustment, used to calculate the total cost impact of the inventory change. Sourced from the inventory valuation layer in SAP S/4HANA. Business-confidential as it reflects supplier pricing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when the adjustment record was most recently modified. Tracks approval workflow updates, status changes, and corrections. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Records all inventory quantity adjustments at the SKU-location level including shrinkage events, damage write-downs, cycle count corrections, receiving variances, expiry write-offs, and RTVs. Captures adjustment reason category, sub-reason (e.g., shoplifting, employee theft, admin error for shrinkage), adjusted quantity, cost impact, detection method, authorizing user, approval status, and loss prevention case reference where applicable. Feeds the stock_ledger and serves as the SSOT for all non-transactional inventory changes and shrinkage monitoring.'
}

Table inventory.stock_transfer {
  stock_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each inter-node stock transfer record in the inventory management system.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider responsible for transporting the stock transfer between nodes. Used for carrier performance analytics and SLA compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Transfer operations incur labor and freight costs allocated to cost centers for P&L tracking. Stock_transfer needs cost_center_id to charge transfer costs to responsible departments and measure transf']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Transfer accounting and intercompany pricing require cost data for inventory valuation during movement. Store-to-store transfers, DC-to-store shipments, and cross-dock operations need cost basis for f']
  destination_node_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or vendor location) to which the stock transfer is directed. Used for inventory receipt confirmation and network replenishment tracking.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Ship-from-store fulfillment: a fulfillment order for ship-from-store triggers a stock transfer between the store inventory node and the outbound staging area. Linking stock_transfer to fulfillment_ord']
  inventory_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inventory_transfer. Business justification: inventory.stock_transfer and supplychain.inventory_transfer represent the same business concept from different system layers (IMS vs. SCM execution). Linking them enables cross-system transfer quantit']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Inter-node stock transfers in retail generate journal entries for inter-company or inter-cost-center inventory transfers. Finance requires traceability from stock_transfer to its GL posting for period']
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.outbound_shipment. Business justification: A stock_transfer (DC-to-store or store-to-store) is physically executed as an outbound_shipment from the DC. Linking stock_transfer to outbound_shipment enables transfer-in-transit tracking and carrie']
  primary_stock_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or vendor location) from which the stock transfer is dispatched. Supports network balancing and omnichannel fulfillment analytics.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional inventory redistribution tracking: retailers execute inter-node stock transfers to position inventory at high-traffic stores ahead of campaign launches. Direct campaign linkage on stock_tr']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order that triggered this stock transfer, applicable for DC-to-store replenishment flows sourced from supplier inbound receipts. Links transfer to procurement context.']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Stock transfers between nodes are frequently triggered by replenishment orders — when a store needs restocking from a DC, a replenishment order is raised and a stock transfer is initiated. Linking sto']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the replenishment plan that generated this stock transfer, linking the physical movement to the demand-driven planning signal from Blue Yonder or Oracle Retail. Supports replenishment accuracy analytics.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being transferred. The SKU is the atomic unit of inventory tracking, uniquely identifying a product variant (size, color, style) within the retail assortment.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Inter-node stock transfers require UOM master reference to ensure transfer_quantity is expressed in the correct unit for both source and destination nodes. Retail DC-to-store and store-to-store transf']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: stock_transfer has is_vendor_managed flag but no vendor reference. VMI replenishment transfers must be attributed to the managing vendor for chargeback processing, VMI settlement, and vendor complianc']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Compared against expected_receipt_date to measure carrier and DC performance against SLA targets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this stock transfer record was first persisted in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transfer cost is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.']
  expected_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date by which the stock transfer is expected to arrive at the destination node. Used for replenishment planning, weeks of supply (WOS) calculations, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock transfer record was first created in the system, representing the business event of transfer initiation. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the transfer lifecycle.']
  inventory_cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) value per unit for the transferred SKU at time of transfer, used for inventory valuation, GMROI calculations, and financial reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO standard cost or moving average cost.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is executed as a cross-docking (cross-dock) movement, where inventory is transferred directly from inbound to outbound at the DC without putaway storage. Supports DC throughput and flow-through analytics.']
  is_ship_from_store boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a ship-from-store (SFS) omnichannel fulfillment flow, where a retail store acts as a fulfillment node dispatching inventory to satisfy an e-commerce or BOPIS order.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program, where the supplier initiates and manages the replenishment transfer rather than the retailer\'s internal planning team.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments associated with the stock transfer, capturing exception details, special handling instructions, or carrier communication relevant to the transfer execution.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the stock transfer, influencing pick sequencing, carrier selection, and dispatch scheduling at the DC or store. URGENT transfers may be triggered by stockout alerts or product recalls.']
  received_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of units confirmed received at the destination node upon delivery. May differ from transfer_quantity due to transit damage, shrinkage, or carrier discrepancies. Drives inventory reconciliation and shrinkage monitoring.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer was tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility during transit. Supports RFID-based inventory accuracy programs.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically dispatched from the source node. Used for lead time calculation, carrier SLA tracking, and inventory in-transit reporting.']
  source_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the originating inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV or DSD; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this stock transfer record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across the retail technology stack.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned shipment tracking number for the stock transfer, enabling real-time in-transit visibility and last-mile delivery monitoring. Used for exception management and carrier SLA validation.']
  transfer_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total logistics cost incurred for executing this stock transfer, including carrier freight charges, handling fees, and any 3PL surcharges. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), P&L allocation, and supply chain cost optimization.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number for the stock transfer, used for tracking and communication across supply chain partners, carriers, and store operations. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM transfer order numbering.']
  transfer_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU authorized and dispatched in this stock transfer. Represents the planned transfer quantity as recorded at time of transfer creation.']
  transfer_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. REPLENISHMENT: routine inventory restocking; NETWORK_BALANCE: lateral balancing to address overstock/understock; RTV: return to vendor; RECALL: product safety recall per CPSC; DAMAGE_RETURN: return of damaged goods; OMNICHANNEL: ship-from-store or BOPIS fulfillment support.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stock transfer. DRAFT: initiated but not yet dispatched; IN_TRANSIT: shipment dispatched from source node; RECEIVED: confirmed receipt at destination node; CANCELLED: transfer voided before completion; EXCEPTION: discrepancy or damage reported during transit.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inter-node inventory movement. DC_TO_STORE: distribution center replenishment to store; STORE_TO_STORE: lateral balancing between stores; STORE_TO_DC: reverse logistics or consolidation; CROSS_DOCK: direct transfer without storage per cross-docking (DSD) model; RTV: Return to Vendor; MFC_TO_STORE: micro-fulfillment center to store for omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store).']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the transferred SKU, used for scanning and verification during pick, pack, and receipt operations at DC and store nodes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this stock transfer record, such as status updates, quantity adjustments, or receipt confirmations. Supports change data capture and audit compliance.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'Difference between transfer_quantity and received_quantity, representing units lost, damaged, or unaccounted for during transit. Positive value indicates shortage; negative indicates overage. Drives shrinkage monitoring and carrier chargeback processes.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining the cause of any quantity variance between dispatched and received units. Used for shrinkage root cause analysis, carrier chargeback (RMA) processing, and inventory reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Inter-node stock transfer records capturing movement of inventory between stores, DCs, MFCs, and vendor locations. Captures transfer type (store-to-store, DC-to-store, cross-dock, RTV), source node, destination node, SKU, transfer quantity, shipment date, expected receipt date, actual receipt date, carrier, and transfer status. Supports omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store) and network balancing.'
}

Table inventory.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each goods receipt record in the inventory domain. Primary key for the goods_receipt data product.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Three-way match (PO + GR + AP Invoice) is a core retail AP process. Linking goods_receipt to ap_invoice enables payment authorization, invoice-to-receipt reconciliation, and chargeback processing. Ret']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: Buyers monitor receipt performance (on-time, quantity accuracy, quality issues) for their purchase orders. Receipt variance resolution and vendor performance scorecarding are buyer responsibilities. B']
  buying_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buying_order. Business justification: Receiving discrepancy reporting against the merchandising buy plan: retail operations match goods receipts to the buying_order to validate received quantities vs. ordered quantities at the merchandisi']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier. Business justification: Carrier performance reporting: goods receipts record the inbound carrier. Replacing the denormalized carrier_name with a proper FK to fulfillment.carrier enables carrier on-time delivery and damage-ra']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving operations are cost centers with labor, equipment, and overhead costs tracked for budgeting and efficiency analysis. Goods_receipt needs cost_center_id to allocate receiving costs and measur']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Receiving process validates invoice costs against contracted pricing and updates landed cost. Three-way match (PO-receipt-invoice) depends on linking goods receipt to cost structure for variance detec']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: DSD (direct store delivery) receipts are received into specific store departments. Department-level receiving reports, DSD reconciliation, and department receiving compliance tracking are named retail']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Goods receipts must be matched to financial periods for uninvoiced receipts accrual accounting in retail. Finance teams use this for period-end GR/IR reconciliation and to ensure inventory asset posti']
  inbound_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled inbound dock appointment at the receiving node. Links to the inbound_appointment product for dock scheduling, carrier arrival tracking, and receiving labor planning.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Goods receipts are triggered by inbound shipment arrivals. Linking goods_receipt to inbound_shipment enables 3-way match (PO/shipment/receipt), carrier performance tracking, and discrepancy resolution']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location (DC, store, or MFC) where the merchandise was received. Supports inventory accuracy at point of induction and omnichannel fulfillment visibility.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Goods receipt triggers GR/IR clearing journal entry (inventory asset debit, GR/IR liability credit) in retail accounting. Finance teams require direct traceability from goods_receipt to journal_entry ']
  lead_time_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.lead_time_agreement. Business justification: Goods receipt must be validated against the lead time SLA to determine on-time delivery compliance, trigger chargebacks for late deliveries, and update vendor scorecards. Retail DC receiving operation']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific purchase order line item being received. Enables line-level PO matching, partial receipt tracking, and SKU-level discrepancy analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. Enables PO-to-receipt matching, open order tracking, and three-way match (PO, receipt, invoice) for accounts payable.']
  receiving_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.receiving_event. Business justification: goods_receipt (inventory system) and receiving_event (WMS) represent the same physical DC receiving event from different system layers. Linking them enables cross-system quantity reconciliation and di']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Goods receipts are created when replenishment stock physically arrives at a node. The goods_receipt description explicitly states it records receipts \'against purchase orders, replenishment...\' — conf']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being received. Links to the product master in Informatica MDM or Oracle Retail Merchandising System for item-level inventory induction.']
  stock_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_transfer. Business justification: When inventory is transferred between nodes (stock_transfer), a goods receipt is created at the destination node to confirm physical arrival. The goods_receipt is the child record that documents the p']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Goods receipt processes validate received_qty against expected_qty in the correct UOM. Retail receiving teams use UOM master to convert between vendor pack UOM and inventory UOM. Required for discrepa']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor from whom the merchandise was received. Links to the supplier master record in Informatica MDM. Used for vendor compliance chargeback processing and fill rate analysis.']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_item. Business justification: Goods receipt validation requires vendor item spec (GTIN, UPC, pack config) to detect pack discrepancies, trigger chargebacks, and perform quality inspection against agreed item specifications. Retail']
  accepted_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units accepted into inventory after quality inspection. Excludes rejected, damaged, or wrong-SKU units. This is the quantity inducted into on-hand inventory and used for COGS valuation.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number from the carrier\'s shipping documentation. Used for shipment tracking, freight audit, carrier dispute resolution, and customs compliance for international shipments.']
  chargeback_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt record has identified discrepancies that qualify for a vendor compliance chargeback. True = chargeback may be issued to supplier; False = no chargeback applicable. Drives the vendor compliance chargeback workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this goods receipt record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit cost and total receipt cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency retail operations and financial reporting.']
  discrepancy_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the discrepancy resolution workflow. \'pending\' = discrepancy logged, not yet actioned; \'under_review\' = being investigated with supplier; \'resolved\' = discrepancy closed; \'chargeback_issued\' = vendor compliance chargeback raised; \'waived\' = discrepancy accepted without penalty.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary discrepancy identified during receiving. \'shortage\' = fewer units than expected; \'overage\' = more units than expected; \'wrong_sku\' = incorrect item received; \'damage\' = merchandise physically damaged; \'expired\' = past sell-by or use-by date; \'none\' = no discrepancy. Drives chargeback and resolution workflows.']
  expected_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units expected at the receiving node based on the ASN or replenishment plan. May differ from ordered quantity due to partial shipments or split deliveries. Used for receiving dock planning and discrepancy detection.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which the quality inspection of received merchandise was completed. Used for compliance reporting, CPSC/FDA audit trails, and receiving cycle time analysis.']
  ordered_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units originally ordered on the purchase order line. Used as the baseline for receipt variance calculation, fill rate measurement, and vendor compliance scoring.']
  overage_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units received in excess of the ASN or PO expectation (received_qty minus expected_qty when positive). Overages require disposition decisions and may trigger vendor compliance chargebacks.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known purchase order number as referenced on supplier documentation and EDI transactions. Retained for operational readability, vendor communication, and three-way match without requiring a join.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality inspection performed on received merchandise. \'not_required\' = inspection waived per supplier agreement; \'pending\' = awaiting inspection; \'passed\' = all units meet quality standards; \'failed\' = units do not meet standards; \'partial_pass\' = some units accepted, some rejected. Drives accepted/rejected quantity split.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the physical goods receipt was posted. Used for inventory valuation, vendor compliance reporting, and lead time analysis. Aligns with SAP Posting Date (BUDAT) in the MM module.']
  receipt_method varchar [note: 'Method by which merchandise was physically received. \'dsd\' = Direct Store Delivery from supplier; \'dc_to_store\' = replenishment from Distribution Center; \'cross_dock\' = direct transfer without storage; \'drop_ship\' = vendor direct-to-customer; \'transfer_order\' = inter-node stock transfer. Drives receiving workflow and inventory valuation logic.']
  receipt_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the receiving associate documenting special conditions, discrepancy details, handling instructions, or other observations at the time of receipt. Supports discrepancy investigation and vendor communication.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the goods receipt document, typically generated by the WMS or ERP upon physical receipt confirmation. Maps to SAP Material Document Number (MBLNR) or Manhattan WMS Receipt Number.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the goods receipt workflow. \'draft\' indicates initiated but not confirmed; \'open\' indicates awaiting physical receipt; \'in_progress\' indicates receiving underway; \'completed\' indicates fully processed and inventory inducted; \'cancelled\' indicates voided; \'on_hold\' indicates paused pending resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the physical merchandise was received and confirmed at the receiving node (DC, store, or MFC). Distinct from system audit timestamps. Maps to SAP Posting Date/Time in MIGO.']
  received_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of units physically counted and received at the dock, prior to quality inspection. Includes all units regardless of condition. Used as the gross receipt quantity for inventory induction and discrepancy calculation.']
  receiving_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical location type where goods were received. \'dc\' = Distribution Center; \'store\' = retail store; \'mfc\' = Micro-Fulfillment Center; \'dark_store\' = fulfillment-only location. Determines applicable receiving workflows and inventory induction rules.']
  rejected_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units rejected during quality inspection and not inducted into inventory. Drives Return to Vendor (RTV) processing, vendor compliance chargebacks, and RMA initiation.']
  rfid_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received merchandise was verified using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) scanning during the receiving process. True = RFID scan confirmed; False = manual or barcode-only verification. Supports inventory accuracy at point of induction.']
  rtv_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Return to Vendor (RTV) process has been initiated for rejected units from this goods receipt. True = RTV in progress; False = no RTV initiated. Supports reverse logistics and vendor debit memo processing.']
  shortage_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units short-shipped relative to the ASN or PO expectation (expected_qty minus received_qty when negative). Used for vendor compliance chargeback calculation and fill rate reporting.']
  total_receipt_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of accepted merchandise received (accepted_qty × unit_cost). Used for inventory valuation, COGS posting, and financial reconciliation. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the received merchandise as per the purchase order or supplier invoice. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), gross margin calculation (GMROI), and financial posting to the general ledger. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the received item. Used for RFID/barcode scanning during receiving, inventory accuracy validation, and GS1 compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this goods receipt record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, discrepancy resolution audits, and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Records physical receipt of merchandise at DCs, stores, and MFCs against purchase orders, replenishment orders, or transfer orders. Captures receipt date, receiving node, supplier/source, PO/ASN reference, line-level received/accepted/rejected quantities with discrepancy details (shortage, overage, wrong SKU, damage), unit cost, receipt method (DSD, DC-to-store, cross-dock), quality inspection status, and discrepancy resolution status. Supports receiving workflows, vendor compliance chargebacks, and inventory accuracy at point of induction.'
}

Table inventory.reservation {
  reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inventory reservation or hold record. Primary key for the reservation data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B retail account-level inventory reservation: corporate accounts reserve bulk inventory against credit limits and account-tier entitlements. Account-level reservation reporting and credit-limit enfo']
  cart_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.cart. Business justification: Inventory reservations are created from cart items during checkout to hold stock. Tracks cart-to-reservation lifecycle for abandoned cart inventory release and reservation expiry management.']
  checkout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.checkout. Business justification: Reservations are created during checkout to hold inventory during payment authorization. Links reservation lifecycle to checkout session for payment-hold-release coordination and checkout abandonment ']
  fulfillment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_line. Business justification: Reservations are SKU-level allocations matching fulfillment line granularity. Retail WMS systems reserve specific SKU quantities for specific order lines during wave planning and pick task generation.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Inventory reservations are created during fulfillment order allocation in retail operations. When an online order or BOPIS order enters fulfillment, the WMS reserves specific inventory for that fulfil']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order (OMS order) that triggered this inventory reservation. Populated for BOPIS, ROPIS, ship-from-store, and e-commerce hold encumbrances. Null for non-order-driven holds (quality, legal, regulatory). Links reservation to the order fulfillment lifecycle.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the inventory node (store, DC, MFC, dark store) where the reserved stock physically resides. Central to ATP calculation and omnichannel fulfillment routing (BOPIS, SFS, ship-from-DC).']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Retail operations reserve inventory for loyalty members (VIP early access, tier-based allocation, BOPIS for members, exclusive product launches). Enables member-specific holds, tier-based inventory pr']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Retail omnichannel fulfillment (BOPIS, ship-from-store, curbside pickup) requires tracking which customer profile created each inventory reservation. Essential for customer service, order fulfillment,']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional inventory hold management: retailers pre-reserve inventory for campaign events (flash sales, Black Friday) to guarantee availability. Linking reservation to promo_campaign enables promotio']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) whose inventory is being reserved or held. Links to the product master for item-level ATP (Available to Promise) calculation and omnichannel fulfillment routing.']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Order promising and ATP calculations require current pricing to validate order profitability and margin. Reservations for BOPIS, ship-from-store, and e-commerce fulfillment need pricing context for ma']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the stock_position record at the SKU-node level that this reservation encumbers. Enables direct linkage between the reservation and the ATP calculation on the stock_position product, ensuring accurate real-time inventory visibility.']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: ATP (Available-to-Promise) calculations and order reservation accuracy require UOM master reference for reserved_quantity. Retail omnichannel fulfillment (BOPIS, ship-from-store) depends on consistent']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: reservation has is_vendor_managed flag but no vendor reference. VMI stock reservations must be attributed to the managing vendor for VMI inventory settlement, vendor-managed replenishment triggers, an']
  authorizing_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the business department that authorized the hold (e.g., Quality Assurance, Legal, Compliance, Store Operations, E-Commerce Fulfillment). Supports departmental accountability reporting and hold resolution workflows.']
  case_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the service case, quality investigation, legal matter, or regulatory recall case that authorized a hard hold. Populated for quality_hold, legal_hold, regulatory_recall, and damage_hold encumbrance types. Links to Salesforce Service Cloud case management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp when the reservation record was first written to the data platform (Silver layer ingestion time). Distinct from reservation_timestamp which captures the business event time. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory_cost_per_unit value (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for international retail operations.']
  encumbrance_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the inventory encumbrance. Soft allocations (e.g., BOPIS reservation, ship-from-store allocation, e-commerce hold) are temporary and customer-driven; hard holds (quality hold, regulatory recall, legal hold, damage hold, price investigation) restrict stock from sale pending resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: soft_allocation|hard_hold|quality_hold|regulatory_recall|legal_hold|damage_hold|price_investigation — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the reservation automatically expires if not fulfilled or manually extended. Critical for BOPIS and e-commerce holds where stock must be released back to general availability after a defined window (e.g., 24–48 hours). Null for indefinite holds such as legal or regulatory holds.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Retail fiscal period (year and period number, e.g., 2024-P07) during which the reservation was created. Supports financial reporting of encumbered inventory valuation, OTB (Open to Buy) impact analysis, and period-end inventory reconciliation per FASB ASC 330.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The omnichannel fulfillment method associated with this reservation. BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), ship_from_store (SFS), ecommerce (ship from DC), drop_ship (vendor direct), in_store (POS hold). Null for non-order-driven hard holds.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific business reason for the reservation or hold (e.g., BOPIS_ALLOC, SFS_ALLOC, ECOM_HOLD, QA_FAIL, RECALL_CPSC, LEGAL_HOLD, DMG_INBOUND, PRICE_INVEST). Enables operational reporting and root-cause analysis of inventory encumbrances.']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for the inventory hold or reservation, providing operational context beyond the structured hold_reason_code. Required for legal holds and regulatory recalls per compliance documentation standards.']
  inventory_cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit for the reserved SKU at the time of reservation creation. Used for financial valuation of encumbered inventory, shrinkage reporting, and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO module.']
  is_recalled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reservation was created in response to a regulatory product recall (CPSC, FDA, or other governing body). When true, the hold cannot be released without explicit compliance authorization. Supports mandatory recall reporting and quarantine workflows.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved inventory is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement, where the supplier retains ownership and replenishment responsibility. Affects financial liability and hold authorization workflows.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the reservation, governing queue position in fulfillment workflows and hold resolution SLAs. \'critical\' applies to regulatory recalls and legal holds; \'high\' to same-day BOPIS; \'standard\' to regular e-commerce holds; \'low\' to pre-emptive soft allocations.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing resolution details, disposition instructions, or compliance notes recorded at the time of hold release. Required for regulatory recalls and legal holds to document the resolution rationale for audit purposes.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for releasing a hard hold. \'pending\' — release request submitted awaiting authorization; \'approved\' — release authorized by responsible department; \'rejected\' — release denied, hold remains active; \'auto_released\' — system-triggered release upon expiry or order fulfillment; \'escalated\' — requires senior authorization.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the reservation was released, cancelled, or converted. Null while the reservation is active. Used for hold duration analysis, SLA compliance reporting, and ATP recalculation upon release.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the reservation, used in operational communications, order management, and customer-facing references (e.g., BOPIS confirmation codes). Sourced from OMS or WMS upon creation.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inventory reservation. \'active\' — stock is currently encumbered; \'expired\' — reservation lapsed past expiry without fulfillment; \'released\' — hold manually or systematically lifted; \'cancelled\' — reservation voided before fulfillment; \'pending_release\' — awaiting authorization to release; \'converted\' — soft allocation fulfilled and converted to a shipment.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the inventory encumbrance was created — the principal business event timestamp. For BOPIS/e-commerce holds, this is when the customer completed checkout. For quality/legal holds, this is when the hold was authorized.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units of the SKU encumbered by this reservation. Subtracted from on-hand quantity to derive ATP (Available to Promise) on the stock_position product. Must be positive and not exceed available on-hand at time of reservation creation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this reservation record (e.g., WMS = Manhattan Associates WMS, OMS = Order Management System, ECOM = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, ERP = SAP S/4HANA, MANUAL = manually entered hold). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WMS|OMS|ECOM|ERP|ORMS|CAR|SFCC|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, or slot location within the node where the reserved inventory is physically stored. Enables warehouse associates to locate and pick reserved stock efficiently. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS bin management.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the reserved item. Used for RFID scanning, POS reconciliation, and regulatory recall identification per GS1 standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp of the most recent modification to the reservation record (e.g., quantity adjustment, status change, expiry extension). Used for change data capture (CDC) and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Records all inventory encumbrances that restrict stock from general availability — including soft-allocations (BOPIS reservation, ship-from-store allocation, e-commerce hold) and hard holds (quality hold, regulatory recall, legal hold, damage hold, price investigation). Captures encumbrance type, SKU, quantity, node, start/expiry timestamps, associated order or case reference, authorizing party, hold reason, authorizing department, release status, and resolution details. Single source of truth for all reserved and held stock, enabling accurate ATP calculation on stock_position.'
}

Table inventory.asn {
  asn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Advance Shipment Notice record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  asn_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating inventory node (DC, MFC, store, or supplier facility) from which the shipment was dispatched. Used for inter-node transfer ASNs.']
  asn_supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier.vendor']
  asn_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who originated the shipment. Populated when asn_type is \'supplier\' or \'drop_ship\'. Links to the supplier master record in Informatica MDM.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier responsible for transporting the shipment. Used for carrier performance tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  destination_node_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving inventory node (store, DC, or MFC) where the shipment is expected to arrive. Drives dock scheduling and pre-receiving workflows in the WMS.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory goods receipt record created upon physical receipt of this shipment. Links ASN to the goods receipt for discrepancy detection and inventory valuation.']
  inbound_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_appointment. Business justification: An ASN triggers dock appointment scheduling; the inbound_appointment is created based on the ASN. Linking asn to inbound_appointment enables ASN-to-appointment matching for dock scheduling efficiency ']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: An ASN is the supplier-side notification; inbound_shipment is the carrier/DC-side record of the same physical shipment. Linking them enables ASN-to-shipment matching and on-time arrival tracking — a c']
  lead_time_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.lead_time_agreement. Business justification: ASN expected_arrival_date must be validated against the lead time agreement\'s delivery window and on-time SLA for vendor compliance tracking and chargeback triggering. Retail DC operations use this li']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that this ASN fulfills. Links to the supplychain.purchase_order product for PO-to-ASN reconciliation and receiving discrepancy detection.']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Advance Shipment Notices (ASNs) are sent by suppliers or DCs prior to physical goods arrival, often in fulfillment of a replenishment order. Linking asn.replenishment_order_id → replenishment_order cr']
  stock_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_transfer. Business justification: ASNs can be generated for inter-node stock transfers — when goods are transferred between nodes, an ASN is sent ahead to the destination node to prepare for receipt. Linking asn.stock_transfer_id → st']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date the shipment physically arrived at the destination node dock. Compared against expected_arrival_date to measure carrier on-time delivery performance and lead time actuals.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the ASN as transmitted by the supplier or DC via EDI 856 or API. Used for cross-system reconciliation and dock scheduling.']
  asn_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ASN. \'sent\' = transmitted by sender; \'in_transit\' = shipment en route; \'arrived\' = at destination dock; \'receiving\' = active receiving in WMS; \'received\' = fully received; \'discrepant\' = quantity or SKU variance detected; \'cancelled\' = voided prior to receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sent|in_transit|arrived|receiving|received|discrepant|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  asn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ASN by origin and fulfillment pattern. \'supplier\' = inbound from vendor; \'dc_transfer\' = inter-DC or DC-to-store transfer; \'cross_dock\' = direct cross-dock flow; \'drop_ship\' = vendor direct-to-customer; \'return_to_dc\' = store or customer return to DC.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number issued by the carrier for the shipment. Legal document of title used for freight claims, customs clearance, and receiving verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN record was first created in the system, either upon EDI 856 inbound processing or manual entry. Audit trail for record lineage.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving node. Determines which receiving workflow and dock door assignment logic applies in the WMS.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a receiving discrepancy (quantity overage, shortage, or SKU mismatch) was detected when comparing the ASN to the actual goods receipt. Triggers discrepancy resolution workflow.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving discrepancy detected against the ASN. \'overage\' = more units received than declared; \'shortage\' = fewer units; \'sku_mismatch\' = wrong SKU received; \'damage\' = goods received in damaged condition; \'none\' = no discrepancy.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Assigned dock door at the destination node for unloading this shipment. Pre-assigned during dock scheduling to optimize receiving throughput and yard management.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination node. Drives dock door scheduling, labor planning, and replenishment availability projections.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight payment responsibility terms for the shipment. \'prepaid\' = supplier pays freight; \'collect\' = receiver pays; \'third_party\' = billed to a third party. Drives accounts payable freight accruals.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment is designated for cross-docking (direct transfer without storage) at the destination DC. Triggers cross-dock workflow in the WMS and bypasses putaway.']
  is_rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID tags are present on cartons or pallets in this shipment, enabling automated receiving and inventory tracking via RFID readers at the dock.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'External purchase order number as referenced on the ASN document. Denormalized from the PO for cross-system matching with EDI 856 transmissions and supplier invoices.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number for LTL (Less-Than-Truckload) shipments. Used for freight bill matching and carrier invoice reconciliation.']
  receiving_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when all line items on the ASN were fully received and confirmed in the WMS. Marks the transition from \'receiving\' to \'received\' or \'discrepant\' status.']
  ship_date date [note: 'Date the shipment was physically dispatched from the source node or supplier facility. Used to calculate lead time and measure supplier on-time shipping performance.']
  shipment_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used for the shipment. Drives freight cost benchmarking, carrier selection analytics, and carbon footprint reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truckload|ltl|parcel|rail|air|ocean|intermodal — promote to reference product]']
  source_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the originating node. Distinguishes supplier facilities from internal DC, MFC, store, or dark store nodes to support routing and receiving workflow logic.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this ASN record. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  total_cartons varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons (cases/boxes) included in the shipment as declared on the ASN. Used for dock receiving verification, pallet count planning, and discrepancy detection.']
  total_pallets varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets in the shipment as declared on the ASN. Used for dock door capacity planning, unloading labor scheduling, and yard management.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of sellable units across all SKU lines declared on the ASN. Used for pre-receiving inventory projection and receiving discrepancy detection against goods receipt.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment in cubic meters as declared on the ASN. Used for trailer utilization analysis, DC storage capacity planning, and freight density calculations.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the entire shipment in kilograms as declared on the ASN. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier compliance, and dock equipment planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Enables real-time in-transit visibility and last-mile delivery monitoring via carrier APIs.']
  transmission_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the ASN was received. \'edi_856\' = GS1 EDI 856 standard; \'api\' = REST/SOAP API integration; \'manual\' = entered by receiving staff; \'portal\' = supplier self-service portal.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN was transmitted by the supplier or DC via EDI 856 or API. Distinct from created_timestamp; used to measure EDI lead time and supplier advance notice compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ASN record, including status transitions, quantity updates, or carrier information changes. Supports change data capture in the lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Advance Shipment Notice (ASN) records received from suppliers or DCs prior to physical goods arrival. Captures ASN number, sender (supplier or DC), destination node, expected arrival date, carrier, tracking number, total cartons, total units, SKU-level line details, and ASN status (sent, in-transit, received, discrepant). Enables pre-receiving workflows, dock scheduling, and receiving discrepancy detection against corresponding inventory_goods_receipt records. Received via EDI 856 or API from trading partners.'
}

Table inventory.lot {
  lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory lot or batch record. Primary key for the lot entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Product recall and quality issue management requires category-level aggregation for buyer notification and regulatory reporting. Lot traceability reporting is organized by merchandise category. Catego']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Lot-level costing for FEFO/FIFO valuation and batch-specific cost tracking. Perishables, pharmaceuticals, and regulated products require lot-specific cost basis for accurate inventory valuation, recal']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt transaction that brought this lot into inventory. Links to receiving documentation.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Lot traceability requires linking each lot to the inbound shipment that delivered it. This supports product recall management, FEFO rotation, and food safety regulatory compliance — a mandatory retail']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Lots are physically stored at specific inventory nodes (stores, DCs, MFCs). The lot master currently has storage_location_code (STRING) as a denormalized bin-level reference, but lacks a direct FK to ']
  parent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lot (parent_lot_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that sourced this lot. Links lot traceability to procurement records.']
  receiving_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.receiving_event. Business justification: Lot creation is triggered by the receiving_event (quality inspection results map directly to lot quality_status). Linking lot to receiving_event enables lot-level quality audit trails and recall trace']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU that this lot represents. Links lot-level tracking to product master data.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Each lot contributes to a specific stock position (SKU+node combination). The lot tracks individual batch quantities (available_quantity, quantity) that roll up into the aggregate stock_position. Link']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Lot-level quantity tracking for perishables (FEFO sequencing, expiry management) requires UOM master reference. Retail food safety and FSMA compliance reporting requires consistent UOM for lot availab']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided this lot. Critical for recall management and vendor performance tracking.']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_item. Business justification: Lot traceability for food safety, recall management, and regulatory compliance requires linking each lot to the vendor\'s item specification (country_of_origin, GTIN, certification). Retail food safety']
  warehouse_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.warehouse_zone. Business justification: Lots are stored in specific warehouse zones (temperature-controlled, hazmat, ambient). Linking lot to warehouse_zone enables FEFO-compliant putaway zone assignment and temperature compliance tracking ']
  available_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Current quantity available for sale or allocation from this lot. Decreases as units are sold or reserved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier assigned batch identifier. May differ from internal lot number for vendor-managed inventory scenarios.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'Date indicating optimal quality period. Product may still be safe after this date but quality may degrade. Distinct from expiry date.']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Product certification or compliance code for the lot. Examples: organic, kosher, halal, fair trade, non-GMO. Supports labeling and marketing claims.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or grown. Required for customs and labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lot record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for unit cost. Standard codes include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, etc.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the product lot expires and should no longer be sold or consumed. Mandatory for perishable goods and pharmaceuticals.']
  fefo_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number for FEFO picking logic. Lower numbers indicate earlier expiry and should be picked first to minimize waste.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for the product. May be UPC, EAN, or other GTIN format. Used for cross-enterprise traceability.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when quality inspection was performed on the lot. Required for compliance with food safety and product safety regulations.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Detailed outcome or notes from the quality inspection process. Captures defects, non-conformances, or approval notes.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is a private label or store brand product. True if private label; false if national brand.']
  is_recalled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is subject to a product recall. True if recalled; false otherwise. Triggers immediate hold and customer notification.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is part of a vendor-managed inventory agreement. True if VMI; false if retailer-owned.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the lot or batch. Human-readable code used for tracking and traceability across the supply chain.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lot. Determines whether the lot is available for sale, held for quality inspection, or restricted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|quarantine|hold|released|expired|recalled|disposed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date when the product lot was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf life calculation and food safety compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the lot. Captures special handling instructions, quality observations, or operational remarks.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Quality inspection outcome for the lot. Approved lots are available for sale; rejected lots must be returned or disposed.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units in the lot at receipt. Used for inventory valuation and traceability reporting.']
  recall_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official recall case or reference number issued by regulatory authority or manufacturer. Links to recall management system.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was received into inventory at the destination node. Used for aging analysis and FEFO sequencing.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining until expiry as of the current date. Calculated field used for near-expiry alerts and markdown triggers.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the product in days from manufacture date to expiry date. Used for aging analysis and markdown planning.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that created or owns this lot record. Examples: WMS, ERP, MDM. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse bin, zone, or storage location where the lot is physically stored. Supports pick/pack operations and cycle counting.']
  supplier_lot_reference varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s original lot or batch identifier. Used for traceability back to the supplier\'s manufacturing records.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature zone for the lot. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for this lot. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculation. May vary by lot due to pricing changes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lot record was last modified. Tracks changes to lot status, quantity, or other attributes for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for inventory lot/batch tracking at the SKU level. Captures lot number, manufacture date, expiry date, supplier lot reference, and quality status. Supports traceability for food safety (FDA FSMA) and recall management.'
}

Table customer.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer profile. Primary key for the golden customer record in Informatica MDM.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer\'s preferred or most frequently visited store location, used for localized assortment and clienteling.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer was originally acquired, used for CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) analysis and channel effectiveness measurement.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the customer first engaged with the business, marking the start of the customer lifecycle.']
  cac_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to acquire this customer, including marketing spend, promotional discounts, and channel costs. Used for CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) to CAC ratio analysis.']
  ccpa_opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when CCPA opt-out request was processed, required for compliance audit trails.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has opted out of personal data sale under CCPA regulations.']
  cltv_score decimal(15,2) [note: 'Predicted total revenue value this customer will generate over their entire relationship with the business, calculated using RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) models and predictive analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer profile record was first created in the master data management system.']
  customer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of customer as individual consumer (B2C) or corporate buyer (B2B), including special categories such as employee or VIP.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the customer, used for age verification, lifecycle marketing, and personalized birthday promotions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for customer communication across all channels including e-commerce, loyalty programs, and promotional campaigns.']
  email_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating customer consent to receive promotional emails and newsletters.']
  employee_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer is an employee eligible for employee discount programs.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name or given name of the customer as recorded in the master data management system.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the customer, typically concatenated from first, middle, and last name components.']
  gdpr_consent_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when GDPR consent was granted by the customer, required for compliance audit trails.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR requirements.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-identified gender of the customer, used for personalized merchandising and assortment planning.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the customer\'s most recent interaction with the business across any touchpoint including POS, e-commerce, mobile app, or customer service.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any attribute in the customer profile record.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name or family name of the customer as recorded in the master data management system.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the customer\'s most recent purchase transaction across all channels, used for recency analysis in RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the customer lifecycle journey, used for targeted marketing campaigns and retention strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|new|active|at_risk|dormant|churned|reactivated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level in the loyalty program, determining benefits, discounts, and personalized services.']
  marketing_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has consented to receive marketing communications including email, SMS, and direct mail.']
  mdm_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0-100) from Informatica MDM indicating the quality and reliability of the golden record match and data completeness.']
  mdm_last_match_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last MDM matching and survivorship process execution that updated this golden record.']
  mdm_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the primary source system that contributed the authoritative data for this golden record (e.g., SAP CAR, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, POS).']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the customer. Nullable field for customers without a middle name.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number used for SMS notifications, mobile app authentication, and last-mile delivery coordination.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer\'s nationality or citizenship.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score indicating customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend, scored from -100 to 100.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for customer service, order notifications, and BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) communications.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred method for receiving communications and notifications from the business.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the customer\'s preferred language for communication and marketing materials.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the customer profile, controlling access to services and transactions across all channels.']
  sms_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating customer consent to receive promotional SMS messages and mobile notifications.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer is designated as a VIP for special clienteling services and exclusive offers.']

  Note: 'Golden record for every customer (B2C and B2B) served by Retail. Stores core identity attributes sourced from Informatica MDM including full legal name, date of birth, gender, preferred language, nationality, customer type (individual/corporate), acquisition channel, CAC, CLTV score, NPS score, lifecycle stage, privacy consent flags (GDPR/CCPA), and golden-record confidence score. This is the single authoritative master entity for all customer identity data across POS, e-commerce, and mobile channels.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account. Primary key.']
  assigned_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: In B2B retail, customer accounts are assigned a dedicated merchandising buyer who manages product selection and vendor negotiations on their behalf. This account-level buyer assignment drives B2B acco']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the default billing address for this account. Used for invoicing, credit checks, and payment processing. Null if no billing address on file.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Retail B2B and employee customer accounts are mapped to cost centers for internal charge-back, departmental billing, and cost allocation reporting. Corporate account management and employee discount p']
  default_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.storefront. Business justification: B2B account default storefront assignment: corporate accounts are routed to dedicated storefronts (procurement portals, wholesale channels). Retail B2B operations use account-storefront assignment for']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s preferred or home store location for in-store pickup, returns, and personalized store-based services. Null if no preference set.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: B2B and corporate accounts require contract-specific pricing (volume discounts, negotiated rates, special terms). Account managers assign custom price lists during contract setup. Essential for wholes']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: B2B account price zone assignment: retail accounts (especially wholesale/B2B) are assigned to geographic price zones determining their applicable pricing tier. This drives account-level pricing execut']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer profile who owns this account. One customer may have multiple accounts (e.g., personal and business).']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Retail customer accounts (especially B2B/corporate) are assigned to profit centers for channel-level P&L reporting and revenue attribution. Finance requires account-to-profit-center mapping for segmen']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_program. Business justification: Account enrollment management: retail accounts must reference the specific loyalty program they are enrolled in for benefit application, billing, and tier evaluation. The existing \'loyalty_program_enr']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the default shipping address for this account. Used for order fulfillment and delivery. Null if no default shipping address set.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique account number used for customer communication, statements, and customer service lookup. Distinct from internal account_id.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account. Active accounts can transact; suspended accounts are temporarily blocked; closed accounts are permanently terminated; pending_activation awaits customer verification; frozen accounts are under investigation; dormant accounts have no recent activity.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier or membership level of the account, determining benefits, discounts, and service priority. Standard is base tier; premium, VIP, and platinum offer escalating benefits.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account indicating the nature of the commercial relationship: personal (B2C consumer), business (B2B corporate), employee (staff discount account), or wholesale (bulk buyer).']
  b2b_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account receives B2B contract pricing instead of standard retail pricing. True for wholesale and corporate accounts; false for consumer accounts.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the account was permanently closed. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis and account lifecycle reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount (in base currency) extended to this account for deferred payment or credit purchases. Null for cash-only accounts. Used for credit management and risk assessment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account\'s base currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All monetary transactions and balances for this account are denominated in this currency.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for analytics, advertising, or personalization. True if consented; false otherwise. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  employee_discount_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is eligible for employee discount pricing. True for employee accounts and authorized family members; false otherwise.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase or transaction on this account. Used for recency analysis, dormancy detection, and customer lifecycle segmentation (RFM analysis).']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has consented to receive marketing communications (email, SMS, direct mail). True if opted in; false if opted out. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  marketing_opt_in_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder provided marketing consent. Null if never opted in. Used for consent audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  marketing_opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder withdrew marketing consent. Null if currently opted in or never opted in. Used for consent audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes about the account (e.g., special handling instructions, VIP preferences, service history, escalation notes). Used by customer service and account management teams.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the account was first opened and activated. Used for account age calculations, tenure-based benefits, and lifecycle analytics.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance owed by the account holder (in base currency). Positive values indicate amounts owed to Retail; negative values indicate credit balances. Used for accounts receivable and collections.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred interaction channel for shopping and service. Used for omnichannel routing, personalized marketing, and channel-specific offers.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that is the authoritative source for this account record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail, Informatica MDM). Used for data lineage and master data management.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date when the account was most recently suspended. Null if never suspended or currently active. Used for compliance and risk management reporting.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the account was suspended (e.g., payment default, fraud investigation, customer request, policy violation). Null if never suspended.']
  tax_exempt_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax exemption certificate number for tax-exempt accounts. Null for non-exempt accounts. Required for audit and compliance verification.']
  tax_exempt_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the tax exemption certificate. Null for non-exempt accounts or perpetual exemptions. Used for compliance monitoring and renewal reminders.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is exempt from sales tax (e.g., non-profit organizations, government entities, resellers with valid tax exemption certificates). True if exempt; false otherwise.']
