Business reality: pick task prioritization and routing depend on service level (ove']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: In-store fulfillment pick tasks are executed within specific store departments. Linking pick_task to store.department enables department-level pick productivity reporting, labor budget allocation, and']

  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order.   header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order that generated this pick task. Links the task to the customer order being fulfilled.']

  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Warehouse picking operations require resolving the exact SKU record to access dimensions, hazmat flags, age restrictions, and weight for pick validation. The existing plain-text `sku` column is a deno']

  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exception or issue encountered during task execution. Provides context for exception resolution.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order is being fulfilled. Supports omnichannel fulfillment strategy including store-based and centralized fulfillment.']
  label_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipping label was successfully applied to the package. Critical checkpoint for shipment readiness.']
  original_sku varchar [note: 'Original SKU requested before substitution. Captured when substitution_occurred is true for order accuracy tracking.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the shipment. Determines shipping method, carrier selection, and dimensional weight calculation.']
  packing_slip_printed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packing slip was printed and included in the package. Required for customer order verification.']
  packing_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the packing station where the item is packed. Used for pack tasks and station utilization tracking.']
  pick_task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pick task. Tracks progression from assignment through completion or exception handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|assigned|in_progress|completed|exception|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for task execution sequence. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for queue management and SLA adherence.']
  quality_check_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the quality check performed on the picked items. Ensures accuracy and condition standards before shipment.']
  quantity_picked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual quantity of the item picked by the associate. May differ from requested quantity due to inventory discrepancies or short picks.']
  quantity_requested decimal(10,2) [note: 'Original quantity of the item requested for picking. Represents the target quantity from the order or wave plan.']
  shelf varchar [note: 'Shelf level within the bay where the item is stored. Completes the physical location coordinates for item retrieval.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the associate began executing the task. Used to calculate task duration and associate productivity.']
  substituted_sku varchar [note: 'Replacement SKU provided when original item was unavailable. Used for substitution analysis and customer communication.']
  substitution_occurred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a product substitution was made during picking. Common in grocery and BOPIS fulfillment when original item is unavailable.']
  task_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds from task start to completion. Used for associate productivity analysis and labor standards calibration.']
  task_method varchar [note: 'Picking methodology used for task execution. Defines how items are grouped and picked for optimal efficiency.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the pick task. Used by warehouse associates to reference and track tasks.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment task activity. Determines the workflow and execution requirements for the task.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity being picked. Defines whether picking is by individual units, cases, or other packaging levels.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code barcode for the item. Used for scanning and verification during pick execution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pick task record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  void_fill_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether void fill material was used in the package. Tracks packaging material usage for cost and sustainability reporting.']
  work_zone varchar [note: 'Designated zone within the fulfillment location where the task is performed. Used for zone-based picking and associate assignment.']

  Note: 'Unified operational task record for all fulfillment execution activities including picking, packing, quality checks, and value-added services. Captures task type (pick, pack, QC, VAS), assigned associate, work zone, location (aisle, bay, shelf, packing station), SKU/items, quantity requested vs actual, task method (single, batch, zone, wave), priority, status (assigned, in-progress, completed, exception), start time, completion time, quality check outcome, packing details (station ID, package type, void fill, label applied, packing slip printed), and substitution outcome (original SKU, substituted SKU, customer approval status). Supports wave-based task generation from WMS systems including Manhattan Associates and Blue Yonder. Enables associate productivity tracking, task queue management, and fulfillment throughput analytics across the entire pick-pack-QC workflow.'
}

  pack_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packing task record. Primary key for the pack task entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier. Business justification: pack_task has denormalized carrier_code and service_level columns but no FKs to carrier or carrier_service. Business reality: a pack task prepares a package for a specific carrier. Adding carrier_id F']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Following from pack_task carrier_id FK above, pack_task also needs carrier_service_id FK to normalize service_level. Business reality: packing requirements vary by service level (overnight requires sp']
  fulfillment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_line. Business justification: A pack task packs specific fulfillment line items. Linking pack_task to fulfillment_line enables quantity reconciliation (items_packed_count vs quantity_ordered), exception tracking at the line level,']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment location (distribution center, dark store, or ship-from-store location) where packing occurred.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: A pack task is the packing station workflow for a specific fulfillment order. While pack_task has header_id → order.header, a direct FK to fulfillment_order is the authoritative in-domain parent. This']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order being fulfilled by this pack task. Enables traceability from packing back to the original order.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.outbound_order. Business justification: DC pack tasks package outbound orders for store replenishment. Links packing execution to supply chain order, supports packing productivity analysis by order type, and enables outbound order status up']
  pick_task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the upstream pick task that provided the items for this packing operation. Links the packing workflow to the picking workflow.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record that this packed package will be included in for carrier handoff and delivery.']
  shipment_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment_package. Business justification: A pack task produces or fills a specific shipment_package. Linking pack_task to shipment_package closes the operational loop between the packing workflow and the physical package record, enabling pack']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.wave. Business justification: Wave-level packing throughput reporting: pack tasks are released as part of a wave in DC operations; linking pack_task to wave enables wave completion tracking, labor productivity by wave, and packing']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pack task was assigned to the packer. Used to measure queue time and task aging.']

  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the exception or special handling required. Provides context for exception resolution and audit trail.']

  gift_message_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a personalized gift message was printed and included in the package per customer request.']
  gift_wrap_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether gift wrapping service was requested and applied to this package. Impacts packing time and material cost.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and carrier compliance documentation.']
  insurance_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Declared value of the package contents for insurance purposes. Used to determine insurance premium and claim limits.']
  items_packed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual items (SKUs) packed into the package for this task. Used for productivity measurement and quality verification.']

  pack_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the packing operation was completed and the package was sealed. Used to calculate pack cycle time and throughput.']
  pack_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the packer began the packing operation. Used to calculate pack cycle time and labor productivity.']
  package_height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the package in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']
  package_length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the package in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']

  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging material selected by the packer for this task. Influences shipping cost, protection level, and carrier handling.']
  package_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual weight of the sealed package in kilograms, measured at the packing station. Used for carrier rate calculation and compliance verification.']
  package_width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the package in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']
  packing_slip_printed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packing slip document was successfully printed and included in the package. Required for customer order verification.']
  packing_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical packing station where the task was performed. Used for workload balancing and station performance analysis.']
  quality_check_performed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who performed the quality inspection. Used for accountability and training needs identification.']
  quality_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the quality inspection performed on the packed package. Failed status triggers rework or escalation.']
  shipping_label_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier shipping label was successfully printed and affixed to the package. Required for carrier acceptance.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires recipient signature upon delivery. Impacts carrier service selection and cost.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the pack task. Used for operational tracking, troubleshooting, and communication between warehouse staff.']
  task_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the pack task based on order SLA, shipping cutoff time, or customer tier. Determines task sequencing at the packing station.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the packing task. Tracks progression from assignment through completion or exception handling.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier assigned by the carrier for shipment visibility. Provided to customer for delivery status monitoring.']
  units_packed_count varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units packed across all items. Differs from items_packed_count when multiple units of the same SKU are included.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pack task record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  void_fill_type varchar [note: 'Type of void fill material used to protect items and prevent shifting during transit. Impacts packaging cost and sustainability metrics.']

  Note: 'Operational task record for the packing station workflow. Captures packer assignment, packing station ID, items packed, package type selected, void fill used, packing slip printed flag, label applied flag, pack start time, pack end time, and quality check status. Tracks the transition from picked items to sealed, labeled packages ready for carrier handoff.'
}

Table fulfillment.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for carrier']
  api_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier provides API integration capabilities for real-time rate shopping, booking, and tracking.']
  base_rate_per_lb decimal(10,4) [note: 'Negotiated base shipping rate per pound for standard service level, before surcharges and adjustments.']
  bopis_ready_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports BOPIS fulfillment logistics including store-to-customer handoff coordination.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Internal short code or abbreviation used to identify the carrier in operational systems.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The legal business name of the carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier relationship indicating whether the carrier is available for shipment assignment.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier based on service model: parcel (small package), LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload), last-mile delivery, same-day delivery, or third-party logistics (3PL) provider.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary carrier contact for operational communication and escalations.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary account representative or contact person at the carrier for operational coordination and issue resolution.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary carrier contact for urgent operational issues and coordination.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier service contract expires or is scheduled for renewal.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier service contract or agreement became effective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier master record was first created in the system.']
  cutoff_time_local varchar [note: 'Daily cutoff time in local warehouse time zone (HH:MM format) by which shipments must be tendered to the carrier to qualify for same-day pickup and service level commitment.']
  dimensional_weight_factor decimal(8,2) [note: 'Divisor used to calculate dimensional weight from package dimensions (length x width x height / factor). Common values are 139 for domestic and 166 for international.']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) for automated shipment booking, tracking, and invoicing.']
  extended_area_surcharge decimal(8,2) [note: 'Additional fee charged for deliveries to remote or extended service areas beyond standard coverage zones.']
  fuel_surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current fuel surcharge percentage applied to base shipping rates, typically adjusted monthly based on fuel price indices.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic regions and countries where the carrier provides service coverage. May include restrictions or limitations.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is certified and authorized to transport hazardous materials under Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations.']
  insurance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier offers shipment insurance or declared value coverage for high-value packages.']
  max_height_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum package height dimension in inches that the carrier will accept for shipment.']
  max_length_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum package length dimension in inches that the carrier will accept for shipment.']
  max_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum package weight in pounds that the carrier will accept for shipment under standard service terms.']
  max_width_inches decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum package width dimension in inches that the carrier will accept for shipment.']
  negotiated_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Volume-based discount percentage negotiated off published carrier rates based on annual shipping commitment.']
  rate_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current rate card and pricing terms became effective.']
  rate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current rate card expires and requires renegotiation or renewal.']
  residential_delivery_surcharge decimal(8,2) [note: 'Additional flat fee charged for deliveries to residential addresses versus commercial addresses.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association for freight carriers in North America.']
  service_level_ground boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier offers standard ground shipping service.']
  service_level_overnight boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier offers overnight express shipping service.']
  service_level_same_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier offers same-day delivery service for local or regional shipments.']
  service_level_two_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier offers two-day expedited shipping service.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier requires recipient signature upon delivery as a standard service feature.']
  tracking_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier provides real-time shipment tracking and visibility capabilities.']
  transit_days_zone_1 varchar [note: 'Standard number of business days for ground shipment delivery to Zone 1 (typically local or adjacent regions).']
  transit_days_zone_2 varchar [note: 'Standard number of business days for ground shipment delivery to Zone 2 (typically regional).']
  transit_days_zone_3 varchar [note: 'Standard number of business days for ground shipment delivery to Zone 3 (typically extended regional).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier master record was last modified or updated.']

  Note: 'Master record for carrier and 3PL partners used for outbound shipment execution, encompassing carrier identity, service catalog, and contracted rate cards. Captures carrier name, carrier code, carrier type (parcel, LTL, FTL, last-mile, same-day), SCAC code, EDI/API integration capabilities. Service catalog includes service levels (ground, 2-day, overnight, same-day, BOPIS-ready), transit days by zone, cutoff times, max weight/dimensions, surcharge types, tracking capability, signature required flag, and geographic restrictions. Rate card includes base rates by zone/weight/service, dimensional weight factor, fuel surcharges, residential delivery surcharges, extended area surcharges, negotiated discount percentages, rate effective and expiry dates, and weight breaks. Serves as the single source of truth for all carrier master data, service definitions, and freight cost references within the fulfillment domain. Used in carrier selection logic during fulfillment order routing and freight cost estimation.'
}

Table fulfillment.carrier_service {
  carrier_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier service offering. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier (logistics provider) offering this service. Links to the carrier master entity.']
  base_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base shipping rate for this carrier service in the default currency. Used as starting point for total shipping cost calculation before surcharges and discounts.']
  bopis_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service is eligible for BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) fulfillment scenarios. Used in omnichannel order routing.']
  carbon_neutral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service is certified as carbon-neutral or participates in carbon offset programs. Used in sustainability reporting and eco-conscious customer service selection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier service record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the base rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used in multi-currency pricing and cost normalization.']
  cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cutoff time (HH:MM format, 24-hour) by which orders must be placed to qualify for same-day shipment with this service. Critical for order routing and fulfillment scheduling.']
  cutoff_timezone varchar [note: 'Timezone identifier for the cutoff time (e.g., EST, PST, CST, UTC). Ensures accurate cutoff enforcement across distributed fulfillment centers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this carrier service configuration is no longer active. Null indicates indefinite availability. Used in temporal service availability logic.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this carrier service configuration becomes active and available for order routing. Used in temporal service availability logic.']
  geographic_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Type of geographic restriction applied to this carrier service (e.g., domestic only, specific regions, postal code whitelist/blacklist). Used in carrier eligibility filtering.']
  hazmat_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service is certified to transport hazardous materials. Used in carrier selection for restricted product categories.']
  holiday_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service supports delivery on recognized holidays. Used in delivery date estimation and peak season planning.']
  insurance_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shipment insurance is automatically included with this carrier service. Affects cost calculations and risk management decisions.']
  insurance_max_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum insured value (in base currency) covered by this carrier service. Orders exceeding this value may require additional insurance or alternative services.']
  international_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service supports international cross-border shipments. Used in carrier selection for international orders.']
  max_girth_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum package girth (length + 2×width + 2×height) in centimeters. Some carriers enforce combined dimensional limits beyond individual dimension constraints.']
  max_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum package height dimension in centimeters accepted by this carrier service. Used in dimensional eligibility checks during carrier selection.']
  max_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum package length dimension in centimeters accepted by this carrier service. Used in dimensional eligibility checks during carrier selection.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum shipment weight in kilograms that this carrier service can handle. Used in carrier selection logic to filter eligible services based on order weight.']
  max_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum package width dimension in centimeters accepted by this carrier service. Used in dimensional eligibility checks during carrier selection.']
  perishable_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service supports temperature-controlled or time-sensitive perishable goods (e.g., groceries, fresh food). Used in carrier selection for perishable SKUs.']
  restricted_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this carrier service is NOT available. Used in international order routing and carrier selection.']
  restricted_postal_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of postal codes or postal code prefixes where this carrier service is NOT available. Used in carrier eligibility filtering during order routing.']
  saturday_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service supports Saturday delivery. Used in delivery date estimation and customer service selection.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the carrier service offering (e.g., GROUND, 2DAY, OVERNIGHT, SAMEDAY). Used in carrier selection logic and order routing.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the carrier service offering (e.g., Ground Shipping, Two-Day Express, Overnight Priority, Same-Day Delivery).']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier service. Determines availability for order routing and fulfillment selection.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier service by delivery speed and priority level. Used for service tier segmentation and customer selection.']
  ship_from_store_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service is eligible for SFS (Ship From Store) fulfillment scenarios. Used in omnichannel order routing and inventory optimization.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service mandates recipient signature upon delivery. Impacts service selection for high-value or restricted items.']
  sla_delivery_guarantee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service includes a contractual SLA (Service Level Agreement) delivery guarantee with refund or credit for late deliveries. Used in service selection and customer promise management.']
  sunday_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service supports Sunday delivery. Used in delivery date estimation and customer service selection.']
  surcharge_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of surcharge types applicable to this carrier service (e.g., fuel_surcharge, residential_delivery, remote_area, oversized, peak_season). Used in total shipping cost calculation.']
  tracking_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service provides real-time shipment tracking capability. Used to determine customer visibility options and service selection.']
  transit_days_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of business days required for delivery from shipment date. Used in delivery date estimation and SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculations.']
  transit_days_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of business days required for delivery from shipment date. Used in delivery date estimation and SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier service record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Defines specific service offerings provided by a carrier, such as ground, 2-day, overnight, same-day, or BOPIS-ready services. Captures service code, service name, transit days, cutoff time, max weight, max dimensions, surcharge types, tracking capability, signature required flag, and geographic restrictions. Used in carrier selection logic during fulfillment order routing.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_node {
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the node data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each fulfillment node (DC, store fulfillment center, dark store) operates as a cost center for expense allocation, budget management, headcount planning, and operational cost reporting required for re']
  cost_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_zone. Business justification: Each fulfillment node operates within a cost zone determining freight factors, duty factors, and overhead factors for landed cost calculations. Node-level profitability reporting and cost allocation i']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Fulfillment nodes include DCs. When node is a DC, linking to dc_facility provides full facility details (dock doors, capacity, WMS, zones). Essential for DC-based fulfillment operations, capacity plan']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.location. Business justification: Retail omnichannel operations require linking fulfillment nodes to store locations for ship-from-store capacity planning, store P&L attribution of fulfillment costs, and SLA assignment by store format']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: A fulfillment node (store, DC, dark store) operates within a price zone that governs ship-from-store pricing, BOPIS pricing, and zone-based promotional eligibility. Retail pricing operations require k']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Fulfillment nodes operating as ship-from-store or BOPIS locations generate attributed revenue and operate as profit centers for segment reporting, channel profitability analysis, and omnichannel finan']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the fulfillment node became operational and available for order fulfillment.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the fulfillment node (street number, street name). Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, floor). Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation in fulfillment operations: manual (human-only picking/packing), semi_automated (conveyor systems, pick-to-light), fully_automated (robotic picking, automated storage and retrieval systems).']
  bopis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) fulfillment capability. True if customers can place online orders for in-store pickup at this location.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the fulfillment node is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the fulfillment node is located (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment node record was first created in the system.']
  curbside_pickup_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports curbside pickup fulfillment. True if customers can pick up orders without entering the building.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the fulfillment node was decommissioned or permanently closed. Null for active nodes.']
  delivery_zone_coverage_radius_miles decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum delivery radius in miles from this fulfillment node for last-mile delivery services. Used for order routing and delivery zone assignment.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading dock doors available at this fulfillment node for inbound receiving and outbound shipping. Key constraint for DC and cross-dock operations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the fulfillment node operations team. Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is certified to handle and store hazardous materials per OSHA and DOT regulations. True if hazmat fulfillment is permitted.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment node record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the fulfillment node, used for distance calculations, delivery zone mapping, and route optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the fulfillment node, used for distance calculations, delivery zone mapping, and route optimization.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the fulfillment operations manager responsible for this node. Business reference, not direct PII.']
  next_day_delivery_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports next-day delivery fulfillment. True if orders can be delivered to customers on the day following order placement.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the fulfillment node, used across operational systems for node identification and routing logic. Typically alphanumeric, uppercase.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fulfillment node (e.g., \'Downtown Distribution Center\', \'Store #1234 - Main Street\', \'MFC Phoenix West\').']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the fulfillment node. Active nodes are available for order routing; inactive/temporarily_closed nodes are excluded from fulfillment logic.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment node by its primary operational purpose: distribution_center (DC - regional warehouse), store (retail location with fulfillment capability), dark_store (fulfillment-only location, no customer traffic), micro_fulfillment_center (MFC - automated urban fulfillment hub), cross_dock (direct transfer facility), returns_center (reverse logistics hub).']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays (Monday-Friday) in format HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 08:00-20:00). Used for order cutoff time calculations and fulfillment scheduling.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends (Saturday-Sunday) in format HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 09:00-18:00). Used for order cutoff time calculations and fulfillment scheduling.']
  pack_station_count varchar [note: 'Number of packing stations available at this fulfillment node. Determines packing throughput capacity for ship-from-store and DC operations.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the fulfillment node. Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  pick_capacity_orders_per_hour varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders that can be picked per hour at this fulfillment node under normal operating conditions. Used for capacity planning and order routing decisions.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the fulfillment node address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  primary_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary shipping carrier assigned to this fulfillment node (e.g., UPS, FedEx, USPS, regional 3PL). Used for default carrier selection in order routing.']
  refrigerated_storage_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node has refrigerated storage capability for perishable goods (dairy, produce, frozen foods). True if cold chain fulfillment is supported.']
  ropis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) fulfillment capability. True if customers can reserve items online for later in-store pickup and payment.']
  same_day_delivery_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports same-day delivery fulfillment. True if orders can be delivered to customers on the same day they are placed.']
  ship_from_store_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports Ship From Store (SFS) fulfillment capability. True if this location can pick, pack, and ship online orders directly to customers.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code (e.g., CA, TX, ON). Organizational contact data classified as confidential per business policy.']
  storage_capacity_square_feet varchar [note: 'Total storage capacity of the fulfillment node measured in square feet. Used for inventory capacity planning and space utilization analysis.']
  storage_zone_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct storage zones within the fulfillment node (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, hazmat). Used for inventory placement and pick path optimization.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the fulfillment node (e.g., America/New_York, America/Los_Angeles), used for scheduling and cutoff time calculations.']
  wms_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WMS system instance managing this fulfillment node. Links to the operational WMS platform (e.g., Manhattan Associates instance ID).']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical locations capable of fulfilling customer orders, including stores acting as ship-from-store nodes, dark stores, micro-fulfillment centers (MFCs), and distribution centers (DCs). Captures node type, address, fulfillment capabilities (BOPIS, ROPIS, SFS, dark store, MFC), active carrier assignments, operating hours, pick capacity (orders/hour), pack station count, dock door count, storage zones, node activation status, and geographic delivery zone coverage. Distinct from the store domain\'s store master — this entity focuses exclusively on fulfillment operational capabilities and capacity constraints. Note: PK naming (fulfillment_fulfillment_node_id) contains a double-prefix defect that should be corrected to fulfillment_node_id.'
}

Table fulfillment.exception {
  exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for exception']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier involved in the exception. Relevant for carrier pickup missed or delivery attempt failure exceptions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fulfillment exceptions (damaged goods, mis-picks, customer returns, carrier claims) create operational costs and shrink that must be allocated to cost centers for variance tracking, exception cost ana']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Fulfillment exceptions (out-of-stock picks, damaged goods, substitutions) in retail store fulfillment occur within specific store departments. Linking exception to store.department enables department-']
  fulfillment_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fulfillment order line item affected by this exception. Nullable if exception applies to entire order.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment location (distribution center, store, dark store, micro-fulfillment center) where the exception occurred.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order in which this exception occurred.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Exception financial impact posting: fulfillment exceptions with financial_impact_amount (short-ships, damaged goods, SLA breach penalties) generate journal entries for write-offs or adjustments. Retai']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Fulfillment exception resolution in retail is prioritized by loyalty tier - VIP members receive immediate escalation, expedited replacement, and premium communication. Real-time exception routing syst']
  pack_task_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.pack_task. Business justification: Packing exceptions (wrong item packed, damaged packaging, weight discrepancy, hazmat violation) occur at the pack station. Linking exception to pack_task enables attribution of packing-stage exception']
  pick_task_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.pick_task. Business justification: Picking exceptions (out-of-stock, substitution required, damaged item, wrong location) are a primary exception type in fulfillment operations. Linking exception to pick_task enables root cause analysi']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Proactive customer exception notification: retail operations must notify affected customers of fulfillment exceptions (delays, lost packages, damage) directly. exception.customer_notified_flag and cus']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to returns.rma. Business justification: Fulfillment exceptions (damaged goods, short shipment, mis-pick) directly trigger or are associated with an RMA. Linking exception to rma enables root cause analysis connecting fulfillment failures to']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_case. Business justification: Fulfillment exceptions (damaged goods, delivery failures, inventory shortages) often trigger customer service cases. Linking exception to service_case enables tracking which exceptions resulted in cus']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Operational exceptions frequently occur at the shipment level — carrier delays, lost packages, delivery failures, and tracking discrepancies. exception already links to fulfillment_order and fulfillme']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Fulfillment exception root-cause analysis and product quality reporting require linking exceptions to the specific SKU record. Enables reports on which SKUs generate most exceptions (wrong-pick, damag']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.sla. Business justification: An exception references the SLA that governs its resolution timeline. exception currently stores sla_target_resolution_minutes as a scalar, denormalizing SLA rule data. Adding sla_id FK normalizes thi']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Vendor-caused fulfillment exceptions (mislabeled items, wrong product shipped, quality defects) must be attributed to the responsible vendor for chargeback processing and vendor scorecard updates. Ret']
  warehouse_task_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.warehouse_task. Business justification: Warehouse task exception management: fulfillment exceptions (pick errors, putaway failures, mislabeled items) are raised against specific warehouse tasks; this link enables root-cause analysis, task-l']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures or process changes implemented to avoid recurrence of similar exceptions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this exception record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial impact amount.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer notification was sent regarding the exception.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer was notified about the exception and its impact on their order.']
  detected_at_stage varchar [note: 'Fulfillment process stage where the exception was identified. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: picking|packing|quality_check|staging|loading|carrier_handoff|in_transit|delivery — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Number indicating how many times the exception has been escalated through management hierarchy. Zero indicates no escalation.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'System-specific code representing the detailed exception reason. Maps to internal exception taxonomy and resolution workflows.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the exception circumstances, root cause, and impact. Captured by fulfillment associate or system.']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the exception, used for tracking and communication across fulfillment teams.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the exception in the resolution workflow.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was first detected or logged in the fulfillment system.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of fulfillment exception encountered. Classifies the nature of the operational disruption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock_at_pick|damaged_item|mispick|carrier_pickup_missed|address_validation_failure|delivery_attempt_failure|item_not_found|quantity_shortage|quality_issue|packaging_failure|label_printing_error|system_error — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial cost of the exception in local currency, including refunds, credits, expedited shipping, or inventory write-offs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments from fulfillment associates, supervisors, or customer service regarding the exception handling.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Role or system component responsible for the exception occurrence or resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: picker|packer|quality_inspector|carrier|system|supervisor|customer_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the exception resolution based on customer impact, order value, and SLA criticality.']
  quantity_affected decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units impacted by the exception. Relevant for quantity shortages, damaged items, or mispicks.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Corrective action taken or planned to resolve the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: substitute_item|cancel_line|reroute_order|reschedule_delivery|issue_refund|contact_customer|escalate_to_supervisor|reprocess — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  resolution_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Time elapsed in minutes from exception detection to resolution. Used for SLA tracking and performance analysis.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was resolved or closed. Nullable if exception is still open.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying cause of the exception. Used for trend analysis and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inventory_accuracy|process_error|system_failure|carrier_issue|product_quality|human_error|external_factor — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception caused a breach of fulfillment service level agreement commitments.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number associated with the exception. Applicable for in-transit or delivery exceptions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this exception record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Records operational exceptions encountered during the fulfillment lifecycle, including out-of-stock at pick, damaged item, mispick, carrier pickup missed, address validation failure, and delivery attempt failure. Captures exception type, exception code, affected fulfillment order and line, exception timestamp, resolution action taken, resolution timestamp, and exception owner (picker, packer, carrier, system). Drives exception management workflows and SLA breach tracking.'
}

Table fulfillment.sla {
  sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sla']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping carrier this SLA applies to (e.g., FedEx, UPS, USPS, regional courier). Null if SLA is carrier-agnostic.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: sla defines SLA rules for specific carrier services (e.g., \'FedEx 2-Day must deliver within 48 hours\'). Currently has carrier_id FK and denormalized carrier_service_level column, but no FK to carrier_']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific fulfillment node (store, DC, dark store, MFC) this SLA rule applies to. Null if SLA applies globally across all nodes of the specified node_type.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category this SLA applies to (e.g., perishables may have stricter SLAs). Null if SLA applies to all product categories.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Retail SLAs are routinely configured differently by merchandising season — holiday, back-to-school, and clearance seasons carry distinct promised delivery windows and breach thresholds. Linking SLA re']
  tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: SLA definitions in retail are explicitly scoped per loyalty tier (e.g., Gold members get 2-hour delivery SLA, standard members get next-day). The existing denormalized `loyalty_tier` plain-text column']
  applies_to_holidays boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA rule applies to orders placed on recognized holidays. If false, holiday orders may follow different SLA rules.']
  applies_to_weekdays boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA rule applies to orders placed on weekdays (Monday-Friday).']
  applies_to_weekends boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA rule applies to orders placed on weekends (Saturday-Sunday).']
  breach_threshold_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours before the SLA deadline at which an alert or escalation should be triggered if the order is not yet fulfilled. Used for proactive SLA risk management.']
  breach_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total SLA time window at which an alert should be triggered (e.g., 80% means alert when 80% of allowed time has elapsed). Alternative to breach_threshold_hours.']
  carrier_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cutoff time (HH:MM in 24-hour format, local node time) by which orders must be ready for carrier pickup to meet the SLA. Null if not applicable.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any regulatory, contractual, or business policy requirements that govern this SLA rule (e.g., \'Required by premium membership terms\', \'State law mandates 24-hour BOPIS readiness\').']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code this SLA applies to (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Null if SLA is not country-specific.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for order value thresholds (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Null if order value thresholds are not defined.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this SLA rule is no longer effective. Null indicates the rule is open-ended and remains active until explicitly deactivated.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this SLA rule becomes effective and should be applied to orders.']
  escalation_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule or workflow identifier defining what actions to take when SLA breach risk is detected (e.g., \'NOTIFY_STORE_MANAGER\', \'REROUTE_TO_ALTERNATE_NODE\', \'UPGRADE_CARRIER_SERVICE\').']
  excluded_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific dates (YYYY-MM-DD format) on which this SLA rule does not apply (e.g., peak season blackout dates, facility closures).']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'The fulfillment execution method this SLA applies to: BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), ship-to-home, ship-from-store, dark store fulfillment, curbside pickup, locker pickup, or direct ship. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOPIS|ROPIS|SHIP_TO_HOME|SHIP_FROM_STORE|DARK_STORE|CURBSIDE_PICKUP|LOCKER_PICKUP|DIRECT_SHIP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic applicability of this SLA rule: GLOBAL (all locations), NATIONAL (country-wide), REGIONAL (multi-state/province), LOCAL (specific metro area or node).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA rule is currently active and should be applied to new orders. False means the rule is disabled or deprecated.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Type of fulfillment node this SLA applies to: store, distribution center (DC), dark store (fulfillment-only location), micro-fulfillment center (MFC), vendor, or third-party logistics (3PL).']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the order was placed: web, mobile app, in-store, call center, marketplace, or social commerce.']
  order_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cutoff time (HH:MM in 24-hour format, local node time) by which orders must be placed to qualify for this SLA (e.g., same-day delivery cutoff at 14:00). Null if no order cutoff applies.']
  order_value_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum order total amount (in currency_code) for which this SLA applies. Null if no maximum order value threshold exists.']
  order_value_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum order total amount (in currency_code) required for this SLA to apply. Null if no minimum order value threshold exists.']
  postal_code_pattern varchar [note: 'Regex pattern or prefix defining the postal/ZIP codes this SLA applies to (e.g., \'^90[0-9]{3}$\' for Los Angeles area). Null if SLA is not postal-code-specific.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this SLA rule: CRITICAL (premium/express), HIGH (next-day), MEDIUM (standard), LOW (economy). Used for resource allocation and conflict resolution.']
  promised_hours_to_deliver decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours from order placement to final delivery to the customer. Used for end-to-end delivery SLAs (same-day, next-day, standard ground).']
  promised_hours_to_ready decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours from order placement to when the order must be ready for customer pickup (for BOPIS/ROPIS) or ready for carrier pickup (for ship-from-store). Null if not applicable to this SLA type.']
  promised_hours_to_ship decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours from order placement to when the shipment must be handed off to the carrier. Applies to ship-to-home and ship-from-store fulfillment methods.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal region or state/province code this SLA applies to (e.g., \'US-CA\', \'US-TX\', \'NORTHEAST\'). Null if SLA is not region-specific.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique code identifying this SLA rule (e.g., BOPIS_2HR, SAMEDAY_SHIP, NEXTDAY_DEL).']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the SLA rule (e.g., \'Buy Online Pick Up In Store - 2 Hour Ready\', \'Same-Day Shipping Cutoff\').']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Category of SLA commitment: BOPIS readiness, ROPIS readiness, same-day ship cutoff, next-day delivery, standard ground, express delivery, or ship-from-store. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOPIS_READINESS|ROPIS_READINESS|SAME_DAY_SHIP|NEXT_DAY_DELIVERY|STANDARD_GROUND|EXPRESS_DELIVERY|SHIP_FROM_STORE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last updated this SLA rule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA rule record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Defines SLA (Service Level Agreement) rules and thresholds governing fulfillment performance commitments. Captures SLA type (BOPIS readiness, same-day ship cutoff, next-day delivery, standard ground), fulfillment method, order channel, node type, promised hours-to-ready, promised hours-to-ship, carrier cutoff time, breach threshold, and escalation rules. Used to evaluate fulfillment order compliance and trigger alerts when SLA risk is detected.'
}

Table fulfillment.carrier_rate {
  carrier_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier rate record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier (shipping company) offering this rate. Links to the carrier master entity.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: carrier_rate has carrier_id FK and denormalized service_level/service_code columns, but no FK to carrier_service. Business reality: a rate is pricing for a specific carrier_service offering. Adding ca']
  base_rate_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base shipping rate amount before surcharges and discounts. This is the starting point for freight cost calculation.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the carrier contract or agreement under which this rate is offered. Business-confidential identifier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier rate record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rate record (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipment destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the shipment destination (customer delivery address).']
  dimensional_weight_factor decimal(10,2) [note: 'Divisor used to calculate dimensional weight from package dimensions (length × width × height ÷ factor). Common values are 5000 (cm³/kg) or 139 (in³/lb).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this carrier rate becomes active and can be used for freight cost estimation and carrier selection.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this carrier rate expires and is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no expiration.']
  extended_area_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee for delivery to remote or extended service areas beyond standard carrier coverage zones.']
  fuel_surcharge_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Fuel surcharge rate expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.1250 for 12.50%). Applied to base rate to account for fuel cost fluctuations.']
  is_hazmat_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this carrier service level can handle hazardous materials shipments under applicable regulations.']
  is_international boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this rate applies to international shipments (cross-border) or domestic shipments within a single country.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum shipping charge that will be applied regardless of calculated rate. Ensures carrier covers base handling costs.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier rate record was last modified or updated.']
  negotiated_discount_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'Discount percentage off published rates negotiated with the carrier, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15% discount). Business-confidential contract term.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this carrier rate, including special terms, restrictions, or usage guidance.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipment origin (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  origin_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the shipment origin location (fulfillment center, store, or warehouse).']
  oversized_package_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee for packages exceeding standard size limits (length + girth thresholds vary by carrier).']
  rate_per_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Incremental rate charged per kilogram above the weight break minimum. Used for weight-based pricing calculations.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Source system or method by which this rate was obtained: carrier API (real-time), manual entry, contract upload, rate shopping engine, or third-party logistics provider.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier rate: active (in use), inactive (not in use), pending (awaiting activation), expired (past expiry date), suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate: base (standard published rate), negotiated (custom contract rate), published (carrier list price), spot (one-time rate), or contract (long-term agreement rate).']
  rate_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this rate record. Incremented when rate terms are updated, enabling rate history tracking and audit trails.']
  rate_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or region for which this rate applies. Carriers use zone-based pricing to calculate shipping costs based on origin and destination distance.']
  residential_delivery_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional flat fee charged for delivery to residential addresses (as opposed to commercial addresses).']
  saturday_delivery_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee for Saturday delivery service, which is outside standard weekday delivery schedules.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Type of shipping service offered by the carrier (e.g., ground, express, overnight, two-day, economy, priority, standard). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground|express|overnight|two_day|economy|priority|standard — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  signature_required_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee charged when signature confirmation is required upon delivery.']
  transit_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of business days for shipment transit from origin to destination using this service level.']
  weight_break_max_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum weight in kilograms for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper limit for the highest tier.']
  weight_break_min_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum weight in kilograms for this rate tier. Carriers use weight breaks to define tiered pricing (e.g., 0-5kg, 5-10kg, 10-20kg).']

  Note: 'Contracted and published rate records for carrier services, capturing base rate, dimensional weight factor, fuel surcharge rate, residential delivery surcharge, extended area surcharge, rate effective date, rate expiry date, rate zone, weight break, and negotiated discount percentage. Used in carrier selection and freight cost estimation during fulfillment order routing. Distinct from pricing domain — this is carrier freight cost, not retail product pricing.'
}

Essential f']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order from which the items are being returned.']

Retail operations teams routin']

  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who is initiating the return.']
  redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: When a returned item was obtained via a loyalty redemption (free reward item), the RMA must reference the original redemption to trigger its reversal. Retail operations require this link for the \'retu']
  return_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store or distribution center where the returned merchandise will be received and processed.']

  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: An RMA is raised against a specific shipment. Multiple shipments can exist per fulfillment_order, so shipment-level linkage enables precise carrier return-rate analysis, SLA breach attribution, and sh']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.storefront.   subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.subscription. Business justification: Subscription orders returned require the RMA linked to the subscription for auto-pause/cancellation logic, subscription return rate analytics, and churn management. Retail subscription operations team']
  authorization_date date [note: 'The date when the return merchandise authorization was approved and issued to the customer.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the RMA was authorized, capturing the exact moment of approval for audit and SLA tracking purposes.']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when the RMA was closed and all return processing activities were completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the expected return value amount.']
  customer_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the customer explaining the reason for return or providing additional details about the issue.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the final disposition of the returned merchandise: restock to inventory, liquidate through clearance channel, rework/refurbish, scrap/destroy, RTV (Return to Vendor), or donate to charity.']
  expected_return_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of merchandise expected to be returned under this RMA, calculated from the original order line items.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer must return the merchandise. After this date, the RMA becomes invalid and the return may not be accepted.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) representing the likelihood that this return is fraudulent, generated by fraud detection algorithms.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date when the returned merchandise was inspected to determine its condition and disposition.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the merchandise inspection indicating whether items meet return acceptance criteria.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this return has been identified as potentially fraudulent based on fraud detection rules or manual review.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to this RMA based on customer tier, return value, or business rules.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date when the returned merchandise was physically received at the return location.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the returned merchandise was received, used for SLA tracking and operational metrics.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual refund amount to be issued to the customer after inspection and approval, which may differ from expected return value due to restocking fees or partial returns.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the refund is issued to the customer.']
  refund_processed_date date [note: 'The date when the refund payment was processed and issued to the customer.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return, typically applied to non-defective returns or opened merchandise.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'The logistics method by which the customer will return the merchandise (ship to distribution center, drop at store, carrier pickup from customer location, or direct return to vendor).']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the primary reason for the return (e.g., defective, wrong item, size issue, customer remorse, damaged in transit).']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the reason for return, providing additional context beyond the standardized reason code.']
  return_shipping_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of shipping the returned merchandise back to the retailer, which may be borne by customer or retailer depending on return reason and policy.']
  return_shipping_paid_by varchar [note: 'Indicates which party is responsible for paying the return shipping cost.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'The type of resolution requested or authorized for this return: full refund to original payment method, exchange for different item, store credit, or replacement of defective item.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the return authorization issued to the customer. Used in customer communications and tracking.']
  rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA. Tracks progression from initial request through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|received|inspected|approved|denied|closed|cancelled|expired — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this RMA record (e.g., Salesforce Service Cloud, Oracle Retail, Store POS system).']
  store_credit_issued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of store credit issued to the customer as part of the return resolution.']

  Note: 'Return Merchandise Authorization master record representing the formal authorization issued to a customer to return one or more items. Captures the RMA number, originating order reference, customer identity, return channel (in-store, online, BOPIS), authorization date, expiry date, return reason code, return type (refund, exchange, store credit), total expected return value, and current RMA status (pending, authorized, received, closed, denied). Primary master entity for the returns domain and the anchor for all downstream return processing workflows.'
}

Table returns.rma_line {

Linking rma_line to adjustment enables end-to-end traceability from customer return line to inventory correction — ']
  buying_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buying_order_line. Business justification: Enables vendor quality scorecarding and defect-driven cost recovery. Retail operations track defective returns back to the original buying order (not just customer order) to support vendor chargebacks']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Returns processing requires original landed cost for refund calculations, margin impact analysis, vendor credit determination (RTV), and financial reconciliation. Core operational link between returns']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Returns processing at DC facilities requires tracking which DC received and inspected returned items for disposition routing, inventory restocking, and warehouse operations. Essential for RTV shipment']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Department-level return rate analysis drives vendor chargeback decisions, markdown planning, and shrinkage reporting. Retail merchandising and buying teams require return line items attributed to a de']
  fulfillment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_line. Business justification: Line-level traceability from return to original fulfillment line enables picker/packer quality scoring, defect tracking by associate, lot/serial reconciliation for recalls, and substitution error anal']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_receipt. Business justification: Connects returned item to original receipt for cost basis validation, vendor chargeback processing, and defect tracking to specific inbound shipments. Critical for vendor performance management and qu']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order from which this item is being returned. Links the return to the originating transaction.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Lot-tracked returns (food, pharma, cosmetics) require linking to original lot for recall traceability, FEFO compliance, and quality root-cause analysis. Regulatory requirement for perishable goods. En']
  original_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific line item on the original sales order. Enables precise traceability from return to original purchase.']
  original_pos_transaction_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction_line. Business justification: In-store returns require line-level reference to the original POS transaction line for unit price validation, tax recalculation, and return policy enforcement per item. rma_line has original_order_lin']
  original_sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: rma_line.unit_retail_price is denormalized from sku_price. Retail returns processing and refund calculation require the authoritative sku_price record for the original selling price to validate refund']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent RMA header. Links this line item to the overall return authorization.']
  rtv_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.rtv_request. Business justification: rma_line.rtv_authorization_number is a denormalized string reference to rtv_request. Replacing it with a proper FK enables line-level RTV-to-return reconciliation, vendor credit audit trails, and char']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Return lines must reference the product SKU being returned. The product_description column becomes redundant as it can be joined from product.sku. This links returns processing to the product catalog.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: When an RMA line is processed, the returned SKU must be traced to the originating or destination stock position — especially for VMI, serialized, or lot-controlled items — to update available inventor']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Supplier identifier for the vendor who originally supplied this product. Used when disposition is Return to Vendor (RTV).']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_item.   vendor_promo_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.vendor_promo_agreement. Business justification: When a vendor-funded promoted item is returned, the rma_line must reference the vendor_promo_agreement to calculate vendor chargeback credits and recover vendor funding. Retail accounts payable and ve']
  warehouse_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.warehouse_zone. Business justification: rma_line.warehouse_location_code is a denormalized warehouse zone reference. Linking rma_line to warehouse_zone enables DC returns processing reports showing which zones handle which return types, sup']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the item upon receipt at the returns facility. Determines restocking eligibility and disposition path.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RMA line record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Ensures proper currency handling in multi-currency operations.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final disposition instruction for the returned merchandise. Determines the next step in the reverse logistics workflow (restock to inventory, return to vendor, liquidate, destroy, repair, or donate).']
  extended_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost value for this line (quantity × unit cost). Used for inventory and cost of goods sold adjustments.']
  extended_retail_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total retail value for this line (quantity × unit retail price). Represents the total refund exposure for this line item.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit Global Trade Item Number for the returned product. International standard for product identification across supply chains.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the returned item was physically inspected and condition assessed. Key milestone in the returns processing workflow.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RMA line record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to return line details.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the RMA. Determines the ordering and position of this item in the multi-item return authorization.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this RMA line item. Tracks the line through the returns workflow from authorization through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AUTHORIZED|RECEIVED|INSPECTED|PROCESSED|RESTOCKED|COMPLETED|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  liquidation_sale_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered from selling the returned item through liquidation channels (discount outlets, third-party liquidators, online auctions). Used to calculate net return loss.']
  quantity_authorized varchar [note: 'Number of units authorized for return on this RMA line. Represents the maximum quantity the customer is permitted to return.']
  quantity_received varchar [note: 'Actual number of units physically received back from the customer for this line item. May differ from quantity authorized.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual amount refunded to the customer for this line item. May differ from extended retail amount due to restocking fees, partial returns, or prorated refunds.']
  restocking_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item is in suitable condition to be restocked and resold. True if item can return to sellable inventory.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return of this line item. May be waived based on return reason or customer status.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the customer is returning this item. Used for root cause analysis and quality improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEFECTIVE|DAMAGED|WRONG_ITEM|NOT_AS_DESCRIBED|CHANGED_MIND|SIZE_FIT|DUPLICATE_ORDER|LATE_DELIVERY|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the return reason provided by the customer or service representative. Provides additional context beyond the standardized code.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit identifier for the product being returned. Used for inventory tracking and product identification.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of goods sold per unit. Used for inventory valuation adjustments and margin analysis on returned merchandise.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the returned item. Standard barcode identifier used in North American retail.']
  vendor_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Credit amount expected or received from the vendor for this returned item. Used in accounts payable adjustments and vendor chargeback reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within an RMA, representing a single SKU being returned. Captures the SKU, UPC, GTIN, product description, quantity authorized versus quantity received, unit retail price, unit cost, return reason at the line level, condition code upon receipt (new, opened, damaged, defective), restocking eligibility flag, and disposition instruction. Enables granular tracking of each item in a multi-item return authorization.'
}
