  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this budget record applies. Aligns with the retail fiscal calendar in SAP S/4HANA FI for annual financial planning and investor guidance.']
  forecast_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The latest forward-looking financial estimate for this budget line, updated during rolling forecast or reforecast cycles. Used for forecast vs. prior forecast variance analysis and investor guidance.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to derive the forecast or budget amounts: top-down (executive-driven allocation), bottom-up (cost center aggregation), driver-based (KPI-linked), statistical (model-driven), or zero-based budgeting.']
  gross_margin_projection decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned gross margin (revenue minus COGS) for the budget period and business unit. Supports Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis and financial close reporting.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record has been locked for the period, preventing further edits. True when the period is closed or the budget has been formally locked post-approval.']
  is_reforecast boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record represents a reforecast (True) rather than the original budget plan (False). Distinguishes reforecast iterations from baseline budget entries for variance tracking.']
  lock_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget record was locked to prevent further modifications, typically at period-end close. Supports financial close process integrity and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for financial planners to document assumptions, rationale, or commentary associated with this budget record. Supports audit trail and management review during financial planning cycles.']
  otb_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Open to Buy (OTB) amount representing the remaining purchasing budget available for merchandise procurement in this period. Calculated as planned inventory budget minus committed and received inventory costs.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning period covered by this budget record. Defines the effective close of the budget window for financial reporting and period-end close processes.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning period covered by this budget record. Defines the effective beginning of the budget window for financial close and reporting alignment.']
  plan_version_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the budget record by planning cycle type: original budget (initial annual plan), revised budget (approved amendment), rolling forecast (monthly update), quarterly reforecast, or annual plan. Maps to SAP CO plan version category.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original planned financial amount for this budget line item in the functional currency. Represents the baseline against which actuals and forecasts are compared in budget vs. actual reporting.']
  prior_forecast_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The forecast amount from the immediately preceding forecast cycle, retained for forecast vs. prior forecast variance analysis and trend tracking in financial planning.']
  revenue_projection decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forward-looking revenue estimate for the business unit and channel in this budget period. Supports top-line financial planning, investor guidance, and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) tracking.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget record was formally submitted for approval in the financial planning workflow. Supports submission tracking and planning cycle compliance.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the person who submitted this budget record for approval. Supports submission tracking and financial planning accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this budget record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The absolute difference between planned amount and actual amount (actual_amount minus planned_amount) for this budget line. Positive value indicates overspend; negative indicates underspend. Supports budget vs. actual reporting.']
  variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage variance between planned and actual amounts ((actual - planned) / planned * 100). Used in financial dashboards and P&L reporting to flag material variances requiring management action.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive financial planning record encompassing annual budgets, periodic forecasts, rolling reforecasts, and forward-looking financial projections for retail business units, cost centers, and profit centers in SAP S/4HANA CO. Captures plan version (original budget, revised budget, monthly rolling forecast, quarterly reforecast, annual plan), fiscal year/period, category (CAPEX, OPEX, COGS, labor), GL account detail with line-item granularity (planned/committed/actual amounts per account and cost center), forecast method, variance analysis (budget vs. actual, forecast vs. prior forecast), revenue/COGS/gross margin/EBITDA projections by channel and business unit, approval workflow, and submission tracking. Supports OTB (Open to Buy) integration, budget vs. actual reporting, financial planning cycles, and investor financial guidance.'
}

Table finance.financial_period {
  financial_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the financial period record in the retail fiscal calendar. Primary key for the financial_period data product.']
  comparable_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding financial period in the prior fiscal year used for same-store sales (SSS/Comp Sales) and year-over-year (YoY) financial comparisons. Accounts for 53-week year shifts in the NRF retail calendar.']
  prior_year_period_financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exact same period number in the immediately preceding fiscal year. Used for year-over-year (YoY) financial variance analysis, P&L comparisons, and FASB comparative financial statement preparation. Distinct from comparable_period_id which accounts for 53-week calendar shifts.']
  ap_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts payable (AP) postings are permitted for this financial period. AP periods may be managed independently from GL periods in SAP S/4HANA to support vendor invoice processing timelines and COGS accruals.']
  ar_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts receivable (AR) postings are permitted for this financial period. AR periods may be managed independently to support customer payment processing, revenue recognition (ASC 606), and loyalty program financial settlements.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the budget or plan version associated with this financial period (e.g., \'ORIGINAL\', \'REVISED_Q2\', \'FORECAST_V3\'). Links the period to the corresponding financial plan in SAP CO for budget vs. actual variance analysis and financial planning cycles.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal calendar convention used for this period. The 4-5-4 and 4-4-5 calendars are NRF industry standards that divide the fiscal year into 4-week and 5-week periods to align comparable store sales (SSS) reporting across years.']
  close_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days allocated for completing the financial close process for this period (from period end date to scheduled close date). A KPI for finance operations efficiency; industry benchmark for retail is typically 5–10 business days for month-end close.']
  close_status varchar [note: 'Status of the financial close process for this period. Tracks progression through the month-end or year-end close cycle. \'not_started\' indicates close activities have not begun; \'in_progress\' means close tasks are underway; \'completed\' means the period is fully closed and reconciled; \'reopened\' means a closed period was reopened for corrections.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code (Buchungskreis) to which this financial period configuration applies. A company code represents an independent legal entity within the retail enterprise (e.g., a specific banner, subsidiary, or country operation).']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'SAP CO controlling area code associated with this financial period. The controlling area defines the organizational unit for cost accounting, P&L reporting, and EBITDA tracking within SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this financial period record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and financial close governance per ISO 27001 and FASB standards.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the functional currency of the company code during this financial period (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Governs currency translation for consolidated P&L and EBITDA reporting.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the financial period (inclusive). Defines the upper boundary for transaction posting, revenue recognition cutoff, and financial close. For 53-week fiscal years, the final period end date may extend one week beyond the standard calendar.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024). Retail fiscal years may not align with the calendar year; typically begins in late January or early February per the 4-5-4 or 4-4-5 retail calendar convention.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal year structure (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April–March, or a custom retail variant). Determines how posting periods map to calendar months in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  gl_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether general ledger (GL) journal entry postings are currently permitted for this financial period. Derived from posting_status but maintained as an explicit business flag for integration with downstream systems that check posting eligibility.']
  is_53_week_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fiscal year containing this period is a 53-week year. Under the NRF 4-5-4 calendar, a 53rd week occurs approximately every 5–6 years to realign the fiscal calendar with the Gregorian calendar. Impacts comparable store sales (SSS) comparisons and year-over-year financial analysis.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (periods 13–16 in SAP) used exclusively for year-end audit adjustments, accrual reversals, and FASB-required restatements. Adjustment periods do not correspond to calendar time.']
  is_quarter_end_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the final period of a fiscal quarter, triggering quarterly close procedures, quarterly P&L reporting, and SEC 10-Q filing preparation for publicly traded retail entities.']
  is_year_end_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the final period of the fiscal year requiring year-end close procedures, including balance sheet finalization, retained earnings posting, and annual financial statement preparation per FASB standards.']
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA ledger group identifier that this period applies to (e.g., \'0L\' for leading ledger, \'L1\' for local GAAP ledger, \'L2\' for IFRS ledger). Supports parallel accounting for multi-GAAP retail entities operating across jurisdictions.']
  mm_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether materials management (MM) postings — including goods receipts, inventory adjustments, and COGS postings — are permitted for this financial period in SAP S/4HANA MM module. Critical for inventory valuation and COGS accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary associated with this financial period (e.g., \'Holiday season period — extended close window\', \'Includes 53rd week adjustment\', \'Restatement applied per ASC 606 guidance\'). Used by finance teams for period-specific documentation.']
  period_close_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the financial period was officially closed in SAP S/4HANA FI, preventing further postings. Critical for financial close scheduling, audit compliance, and FASB revenue recognition cutoff enforcement.']
  period_length_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days in the financial period (typically 28 or 35 days for 4-week or 5-week NRF periods). Used for daily average calculations, proration of fixed costs, and lease accounting (ASC 842) period allocations.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the financial period (e.g., \'FY2024 Period 1\', \'Q1 FY2024 Week 1\', \'January 2024\'). Used in financial reports, P&L statements, and EBITDA dashboards for period labeling.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential period number within the fiscal year (1–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special/adjustment periods). Maps to SAP S/4HANA posting period (MONAT) in table BKPF. Controls which periods are open for posting in the general ledger.']
  period_open_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the posting period was officially opened for journal entry postings in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for financial close audit trails and compliance with FASB reporting timelines.']
  period_short_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code uniquely identifying the financial period across systems (e.g., \'FY2024P01\', \'FY2024P13\'). Used as a business key for cross-system integration between SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS), and financial reporting tools.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period by its accounting purpose. \'regular\' periods are standard operating periods; \'adjustment\' periods are used for year-end adjustments; \'closing\' periods are used for financial close entries; \'opening\' periods for balance carryforward; \'special\' for audit or correction postings.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current open/close status of the posting period in the general ledger. \'open\' allows new journal entries; \'closed\' prevents further postings; \'blocked\' restricts posting pending review; \'future\' indicates the period is not yet active. Controls SAP S/4HANA FI posting period management (OB52).']
  quarter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable fiscal quarter label (e.g., \'Q1 FY2024\', \'Q4 FY2023\'). Used in financial reporting dashboards, investor relations materials, and comparable store sales (SSS/Comp Sales) analysis.']
  quarter_number varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1–4) to which this period belongs within the fiscal year. Used for quarterly P&L reporting, EBITDA tracking, and SEC/FASB quarterly financial disclosures.']
  reopen_date date [note: 'The date on which a previously closed financial period was reopened for additional postings or corrections. Populated only when close_status is \'reopened\'. Supports audit trail requirements for FASB restatements.']
  reopen_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for reopening a previously closed financial period (e.g., \'Audit adjustment required\', \'Revenue recognition restatement per ASC 606\', \'Vendor chargeback correction\'). Required for SOX compliance and audit trail documentation.']
  scheduled_close_date date [note: 'The planned/target date for completing the financial close process for this period, as defined in the financial close calendar. Used to track close cycle performance and SLA adherence for the finance operations team.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the financial period. Defines the inclusive lower boundary for transaction posting, revenue recognition (ASC 606), and financial close scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this financial period record (e.g., status change, reopen event, close date update). Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail compliance.']
  week_number varchar [note: 'Fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1–52 or 1–53 for 53-week years). Critical for weekly retail performance reporting, same-store sales (SSS) comparisons, and Open-to-Buy (OTB) planning cycles.']

  Note: 'Fiscal calendar and financial period master defining the retail fiscal year structure (4-5-4 or 4-4-5 calendar), accounting periods, quarter boundaries, and year-end close dates. Includes period open/close status, period type (regular, adjustment, closing), and fiscal year variant. Controls posting periods in SAP S/4HANA FI and drives financial close scheduling.'
}

Table finance.tax_posting {
  tax_posting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax posting transaction record in SAP S/4HANA FI-TX.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Tax-exempt certificate validation and account-level nexus determination require linking tax postings to customer accounts. Retail tax compliance audits verify tax_exempt_certificate_number and tax_exe']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Vendor invoices (AP) are a primary source of tax postings in retail — purchase tax, use tax, and VAT on vendor invoices must be tracked. Linking tax_posting.ap_invoice_id → ap_invoice.ap_invoice_id en']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Customer invoices (AR) generate sales tax and VAT postings in retail operations. Linking tax_posting.ar_invoice_id → ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id enables direct reconciliation of tax collected on customer']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax postings have cost_center (STRING) but no FK to cost_center master. This FK enables cost allocation for tax expenses.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Sales and use tax postings by facility location for nexus determination, tax jurisdiction assignment, and compliance reporting. Required for multi-state retail tax compliance and audit defense.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Tax postings must be associated with a financial period for period-end tax accruals, VAT return filing, and regulatory reporting. The existing reporting_period STRING is a denormalized text field that']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax postings have gl_account_number (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK enables account validation for tax postings.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Cross-border retail inbound shipments trigger import duties, customs taxes, and freight taxes posted directly against the shipment. Tax authorities require shipment-level tax audit trails. tax_posting']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Tax postings in SAP S/4HANA are always generated as part of a GL document posting. Linking tax_posting.journal_entry_id → journal_entry.journal_entry_id provides direct traceability from the tax sub-l']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Tax postings in SAP S/4HANA are ledger-specific — tax amounts may differ between leading ledger (GAAP) and extension ledgers (IFRS, management). Linking tax_posting.ledger_id → ledger.ledger_id enable']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail store location where the taxable transaction occurred (for POS sales tax postings).']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Line-level tax compliance: e-commerce and omnichannel orders require per-line tax postings for multi-jurisdiction nexus reporting and ASC 740 compliance. Retail tax teams trace tax postings to origina']
  original_posting_tax_posting_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax posting ID that this record reverses (populated only when reversal_flag is true).']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction. Business justification: Sales tax compliance: every POS transaction generates tax postings for jurisdiction-level remittance and audit. Tax authorities require transaction-level traceability. A retail tax compliance analyst ']
  pos_transaction_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction_line. Business justification: POS line-level tax audit: each POS transaction line has its own tax_code and tax_rate; tax postings must trace to the specific line for sales tax audit defense, jurisdiction remittance, and tax-exempt']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer for output tax postings on sales transactions.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Tax postings have profit_center (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This FK enables P&L allocation for tax expenses.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Sales tax / VAT compliance: in retail, the taxable event is the shipment of goods to the customer. Tax authorities require traceability from tax postings to the shipment that triggered the tax obligat']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier/vendor for input tax postings on purchase transactions.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to supporting documentation for tax audit defense (original invoice, receipt, exemption certificate, tax calculation log).']
  business_area varchar [note: 'The business area code for cross-company-code financial reporting and segment analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for this tax posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax posting record was first created in the SAP S/4HANA FI-TX system.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known accounting document number from which this tax posting originated (invoice, receipt, credit memo).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of source accounting document that generated this tax posting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert transaction currency to local currency for GL posting.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the company\'s local reporting currency for financial consolidation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax posting record was last modified (for correction postings or adjustments).']
  nexus_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the retailer has established tax nexus (physical or economic presence) in this jurisdiction, triggering tax collection obligation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the tax transaction was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this posting is a reversal of a previously posted tax transaction (for corrections or cancellations).']
  reverse_charge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a reverse charge VAT transaction where the customer is responsible for self-assessing and remitting VAT.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount posted to the tax GL account, in transaction currency.']
  tax_amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount converted to local reporting currency for financial statement consolidation.']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'The name of the government tax authority to which this tax will be remitted (e.g., California Department of Tax and Fee Administration, IRS).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The SAP tax code that defines the tax type, rate, and GL account assignment for this posting.']
  tax_determination_date date [note: 'The business event date used to determine applicable tax rates and jurisdiction (typically transaction date or delivery date).']
  tax_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is input tax (paid to suppliers) or output tax (collected from customers).']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction qualified for tax exemption (e.g., resale certificate, government entity, non-profit exemption).']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The exemption certificate number provided by the customer to support tax-exempt status (resale certificate, government ID, non-profit certificate).']
  tax_jurisdiction_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name for city-level sales tax jurisdictions (applicable in US multi-jurisdiction tax scenarios).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Composite jurisdiction code uniquely identifying the complete tax authority (country-state-county-city combination).']
  tax_jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the tax jurisdiction where the tax obligation arises.']
  tax_jurisdiction_county varchar [note: 'County or district name for county-level sales tax jurisdictions (applicable in US multi-jurisdiction tax scenarios).']
  tax_jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code within the country for state-level sales tax (US) or provincial tax (Canada).']
  tax_point_date date [note: 'The date when the tax liability arises for VAT purposes (typically invoice date or goods delivery date per VAT regulations).']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(7,5) [note: 'The effective tax rate percentage applied to the taxable base (e.g., 8.25000 for 8.25% sales tax).']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'The retailer\'s tax registration number with the jurisdiction\'s tax authority (sales tax permit number, VAT registration number, GST number).']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax being posted (sales tax, VAT, use tax, excise duty, withholding tax, customs duty).']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross transaction amount subject to tax before tax calculation, in transaction currency.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the tax transaction was recorded.']

  Note: 'Tax transaction record capturing sales tax, VAT, use tax, and excise duty postings on retail transactions in SAP S/4HANA FI-TX. Includes tax code, tax jurisdiction (state/county/city/country), taxable base amount, tax amount, tax rate, tax type (input/output), tax determination date, reporting period, nexus indicator, exempt flag, and source document reference. Supports multi-jurisdiction sales tax compliance, Streamlined Sales Tax (SST) reporting, tax return preparation, tax audit defense, and regulatory reporting to federal, state, and local tax authorities.'
}

Table finance.ledger {
  ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ledger']
  parent_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ledger (parent_ledger_id)']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The accounting standard or framework that governs this ledger (e.g., US GAAP, IFRS).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code to which this ledger belongs in the enterprise structure.']
  consolidation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for consolidation scenarios when this ledger is used for group reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'The controlling area code for management accounting and cost center reporting associated with this ledger.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency of the ledger.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ledger ceases to be active for new postings. Null indicates an open-ended ledger.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this ledger becomes active and available for financial postings.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Code representing the fiscal calendar variant used by this ledger (e.g., calendar year, 4-4-5 retail calendar).']
  is_consolidation_ledger boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger is used for group consolidation and intercompany elimination purposes.']
  is_leading_ledger boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary leading ledger for the organization (true) or a secondary/parallel ledger (false).']
  last_closed_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent period close was completed for this ledger.']
  last_closed_period varchar [note: 'The most recent fiscal period (YYYYMM format) that has been closed in this ledger.']
  ledger_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the ledger based on reporting purpose and accounting framework alignment.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the ledger used in financial reporting and system integration.']
  ledger_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the ledger purpose, scope, and usage within the financial reporting framework.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the ledger for human-readable identification.']
  ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ledger in the financial system lifecycle.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger based on its functional purpose in the financial accounting system.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this ledger record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was last modified in the system.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'Code defining the number and structure of posting periods (e.g., 12 regular periods plus special periods) for this ledger.']
  profit_loss_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number used for profit and loss statement aggregation.']
  retained_earnings_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number used for retained earnings carry-forward during year-end closing.']
  supports_cost_center boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports cost center accounting for overhead allocation and analysis.']
  supports_profit_center boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports profit center accounting for internal management reporting.']
  supports_segment_reporting boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports segment-level financial reporting as required by FASB ASC 280.']
  valuation_view varchar [note: 'The valuation perspective or view that this ledger represents for parallel accounting purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this ledger record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ledger. Referenced by ledger_id.'
}

Table supplychain.replenishment_plan {
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each replenishment plan record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the replenishment_plan data product.']
  assortment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.assortment_plan. Business justification: Assortment plans define active SKUs, depth targets, and planned weeks of supply that directly govern replenishment logic. Retail replenishment systems must know which assortment plan authorized each S']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Retail Open-to-Buy (OTB) budgeting requires replenishment plans to consume and track against finance_budget OTB allocations. replenishment_plan.otb_consumed and finance_budget.otb_amount are the two s']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Replenishment order valuation uses the current landed cost price to calculate planned_order_value and assess margin. Retail supply planners reference cost_price for accurate order economics. unit_cost']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand forecast plan from Blue Yonder Demand Planning that drove the generation of this replenishment plan. Enables traceability from replenishment decision back to the underlying demand signal.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply chain node (store, Distribution Center (DC), or Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC)) for which this replenishment plan is generated. Identifies the destination receiving location in the replenishment network.']
  merch_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merch_plan. Business justification: Replenishment plans must reconcile against merchandise plan OTB budgets and planned receipt amounts. Retail S&OP process requires planners to validate that replenishment orders stay within merch plan ']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.plan. Business justification: A replenishment plan for a specific SKU/node is generated within the context of a broader aggregated supply plan. Linking replenishment_plan.plan_id → plan.plan_id provides the parent-child traceabili']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Retail replenishment planners create promotional replenishment plans (event buys / inventory builds) tied to specific campaigns. The existing \'promotion_flag\' on replenishment_plan signals this need; ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Replenishment plans in retail are season-scoped — seasonal items have distinct reorder points, safety stock levels, and plan horizon dates tied to season buy deadlines and clearance exit dates. Season']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this replenishment plan is generated. The SKU is the primary product-level identifier used in replenishment planning across stores, DCs, and MFCs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary supplier responsible for fulfilling this replenishment plan. Links to the supplier master for MOQ enforcement, lead time, and EDI ordering constraints.']
  approved_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final order quantity approved by the buyer or auto-approved by the system after MOQ, order multiple, and buyer override adjustments. This is the quantity that will be transmitted to the supplier via EDI or purchase order.']
  buyer_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a buyer has manually overridden the system-generated planned order quantity or parameters. True = buyer override applied; False = system-generated values accepted as-is. Supports audit trail for manual interventions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this replenishment plan record was first persisted in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the planned order value and unit cost are expressed (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency retail operations.']
  current_on_hand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU physically on hand at the supply node at the time the replenishment plan was generated. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS or SAP S/4HANA WM. Used as the starting inventory position for plan calculations.']
  demand_variability_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'A statistical measure of demand variability (coefficient of variation or standard deviation of demand) used by the planning engine to size safety stock. Higher values indicate more volatile demand and result in larger safety stock buffers.']
  fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target order fill rate percentage (Fill Rate) for this replenishment plan, representing the percentage of demand that should be fulfilled from available inventory. Used as a supply chain performance KPI (KPI) target for this SKU-node combination.']
  forecasted_demand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total forecasted demand quantity for the SKU at the supply node over the plan horizon, as generated by Blue Yonder Demand Planning. The primary input to the planned order quantity calculation.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected receipt at the supply node, as defined in the supplier contract or purchasing info record. Used to calculate reorder points and plan order release dates.']
  min_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum monetary value of a purchase order required by the supplier, expressed in the operating currency. Replenishment plans that do not meet the minimum order value threshold are consolidated or flagged for buyer action.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum number of units that must be ordered from the supplier per purchase order line, as enforced by supplier contract terms. Replenishment plans with planned_order_qty below MOQ are rounded up or flagged for buyer review.']
  moq_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned order quantity meets or exceeds the supplier\'s Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraint. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold and requires buyer review or order consolidation before release.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of supply chain node receiving the replenishment. Values: store (retail store), dc (Distribution Center), mfc (Micro-Fulfillment Center), dark_store (fulfillment-only location). Drives replenishment logic and lead time parameters.']
  on_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU already on open purchase orders or in-transit to the supply node at the time of plan generation. Included in the net inventory position calculation to avoid over-ordering.']
  order_multiple decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit increment in which the SKU must be ordered (e.g., case pack size, pallet quantity). Planned order quantities are rounded up to the nearest order multiple before purchase order generation.']
  order_release_date date [note: 'The date on which the replenishment plan is scheduled to be released as a purchase order to the supplier. Calculated by subtracting lead time from the planned receipt date. Drives the purchasing execution calendar.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why a buyer manually overrode the system-generated replenishment plan. Populated only when buyer_override_flag is True. Supports root cause analysis of planning accuracy and buyer behavior patterns.']
  plan_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment plan was generated by the planning engine (Blue Yonder or equivalent). Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Used for plan freshness assessment and demand signal alignment.']
  plan_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning horizon covered by this replenishment plan. Defines the end of the demand forecast window. Together with plan_horizon_start_date, bounds the coverage period for this plan.']
  plan_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning horizon covered by this replenishment plan. Defines the beginning of the demand forecast window used to generate the planned order quantity.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the replenishment plan within the planning and execution workflow. Drives automated ordering triggers and buyer review queues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|proposed|approved|released|in_progress|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the origin and nature of the replenishment plan. Automated plans are system-generated by Blue Yonder; manual plans are buyer-created; override plans supersede automated recommendations; emergency plans address urgent stockout risk.']
  planned_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of units recommended by the planning engine to be ordered for this SKU at the specified supply node. Subject to MOQ and order multiple enforcement before release to purchasing.']
  planned_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the planned replenishment order, calculated as planned_order_qty multiplied by unit_cost. Used for Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget management and financial planning.']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'The expected date on which the replenishment order is planned to be received at the supply node, calculated as the order release date plus supplier lead time. Used for inventory projection and DC scheduling.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment plan was generated or adjusted to support a planned promotional event. True = promotion-driven uplift included in forecasted demand; False = baseline replenishment. Links to promotional demand lift in Blue Yonder.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'The inventory level (in units) at the supply node at which a replenishment order is triggered. Calculated as safety stock plus expected demand during supplier lead time. Core parameter for reorder-point-based replenishment policies.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'The replenishment policy calculation method applied to generate this plan. Min/Max triggers orders when inventory falls below minimum; Reorder Point (ROP) uses statistical triggers; Days of Supply targets coverage duration; Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) delegates to supplier; Cross-Docking bypasses storage; Direct Store Delivery (DSD) is supplier-direct. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: min_max|reorder_point|days_of_supply|vendor_managed|cross_dock|dsd — promote to reference product]']
  safety_stock_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the safety stock quantity. Fixed uses a static buffer; Statistical uses demand variability and lead time variance; Days of Supply targets a coverage duration; Service Level Based derives buffer from the target service level percentage.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The buffer stock quantity maintained at the supply node to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Represents the minimum inventory floor below which replenishment is urgently triggered. Also referred to as buffer stock.']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target in-stock service level percentage used to calculate safety stock and replenishment parameters. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Represents the probability of not experiencing a stockout during the replenishment lead time.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that generated or last updated this replenishment plan. Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking. Values: blue_yonder (Blue Yonder Demand Planning), sap_s4hana (SAP S/4HANA MM), orms (Oracle Retail Merchandising System), manual (buyer-created).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this replenishment plan record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Tracks buyer overrides, status transitions, and plan revisions.']
  weeks_of_supply_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target number of weeks of inventory coverage (Weeks of Supply / WOS) that the replenishment plan aims to achieve at the supply node after order receipt. Used as a planning horizon parameter for days-of-supply replenishment methods.']

  Note: 'Replenishment plans for SKUs across all supply chain nodes (stores, DCs, MFCs), generated by Blue Yonder or equivalent planning engine. Captures planned order quantities, replenishment triggers, reorder points, safety stock levels and policy parameters (calculation method, service level target, demand variability factor), weeks-of-supply targets, plan status, and MOQ enforcement rules (minimum order quantity, order multiples, minimum order value). SSOT for automated replenishment decisions including buffer stock governance and supplier ordering constraints.'
}

Table supplychain.demand_forecast {
  demand_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SKU-level demand forecast record generated by Blue Yonder Demand Planning. Serves as the primary key for this table.']
  assortment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.assortment. Business justification: Assortment-scoped demand forecasting: demand forecasts are only generated for SKU-location combinations that are active in the assortment. Linking demand_forecast to assortment enables planners to val']
  assortment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.assortment_plan. Business justification: New item demand forecasting (is_new_item flag on demand_forecast) is seeded from assortment plans — the assortment_plan defines planned_sku_count and planned_units for new items. Retail demand planner']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Segment-driven demand planning: retail demand planners adjust forecasts based on customer segment behavior (churn risk, purchase frequency, AOV). Linking demand_forecast to the driving customer segmen']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store, distribution center (DC), or fulfillment node for which the demand forecast applies. Supports store-level and DC-level replenishment planning.']
  merch_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merch_plan. Business justification: Demand forecasts must reconcile against merchandise plan planned_sales_amount and planned_units as top-down targets. Retail S&OP requires demand planners to show forecast vs. merch plan variance. This']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Retail demand planners build promotional demand forecasts tied to specific campaigns to model promotional lift units. The \'promotional_lift_units\' and \'is_promotional_period\' attributes on demand_fore']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Demand forecasts in retail are inherently seasonal — seasonality_index, forecast horizon, and promotional lift are all season-driven. Linking demand_forecast to season enables season-level forecast ac']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which the demand forecast is generated. The SKU is the atomic unit of inventory and demand planning in retail.']
  baseline_forecast_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The pure statistical baseline demand forecast in units before any adjustments for promotions, seasonality, or manual overrides. Provides the unadjusted demand signal for comparison against the final consensus forecast.']
  confidence_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The statistical confidence level (expressed as a percentage, e.g., 95.00) associated with the forecast interval. Indicates the probability that actual demand will fall within the forecast range. Used to set safety stock levels and replenishment buffers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this demand forecast record was first written to the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and incremental load processing.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the forecasted revenue amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that provided the demand signal inputs used to generate this forecast (e.g., SAP_CAR for POS transaction data, Salesforce_Commerce_Cloud for e-commerce demand, ORMS for inventory positions). Supports data lineage and source system auditability.']
  demand_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the SKU\'s demand pattern used to select the appropriate forecasting model. \'regular\' indicates stable, predictable demand; \'intermittent\' indicates sporadic demand (Croston model); \'lumpy\' indicates irregular high-volume spikes; \'new_item\' indicates insufficient history; \'end_of_life\' indicates declining/discontinuing SKU.']
  forecast_bias decimal(8,4) [note: 'The systematic directional error in the forecast, calculated as the mean of (forecasted units minus actual units) over a rolling period. Positive values indicate over-forecasting (excess inventory risk); negative values indicate under-forecasting (stockout risk). Critical for replenishment safety stock calibration.']
  forecast_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this forecast record was generated by the Blue Yonder Demand Planning engine. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the forecast lifecycle and supports version comparison across planning cycles.']
  forecast_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'The total number of weeks into the future covered by this forecast run, measured from the forecast generation date. Determines the planning horizon for replenishment, Open-to-Buy (OTB), and supply chain optimization.']
  forecast_lower_bound_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The lower bound of the forecast confidence interval in units at the specified confidence level. Represents the low-demand scenario used for minimum order quantity (MOQ) planning and markdown risk assessment.']
  forecast_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the forecast horizon period covered by this record. Together with forecast_period_start_date, defines the time bucket granularity (daily, weekly, monthly).']
  forecast_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the forecast horizon period covered by this record. Defines the beginning of the time bucket (day, week, or month) for which demand is forecasted.']
  forecast_run_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific Blue Yonder Demand Planning batch run that generated this forecast record. Enables traceability back to the originating planning cycle and supports audit of forecast lineage.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the demand forecast record. \'draft\' indicates a preliminary run; \'published\' indicates the forecast is active for replenishment; \'approved\' indicates planner sign-off; \'overridden\' indicates a manual adjustment was applied; \'superseded\' indicates a newer run has replaced this record; \'archived\' indicates the record is retained for historical analysis only.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand forecast by its planning purpose. \'baseline\' is the statistical base demand without uplift; \'promotional\' incorporates promotion lift; \'seasonal\' reflects seasonal index adjustments; \'event_driven\' accounts for specific demand events; \'consensus\' is the final agreed forecast used for replenishment and Open-to-Buy (OTB) planning.']
  forecast_upper_bound_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The upper bound of the forecast confidence interval in units at the specified confidence level. Represents the high-demand scenario used for safety stock and maximum inventory planning to prevent stockouts.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'An incrementing integer version number for the forecast record for a given SKU-location-period combination. Enables tracking of forecast revisions across planning cycles and supports comparison of forecast evolution over time.']
  forecasted_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The projected revenue in the operating currency derived from the forecasted units multiplied by the expected selling price for the forecast period. Used in financial planning, Open-to-Buy (OTB) budgeting, and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis.']
  forecasted_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The statistical model\'s predicted demand quantity in sellable units for the SKU at the specified location during the forecast period. This is the primary quantitative output of the demand planning engine and drives replenishment order quantities.']
  is_latest_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast record is the most current version for the given SKU-location-period combination. True means this record is the active forecast used for replenishment and OTB planning; False means it has been superseded by a newer forecast run.']
  is_new_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is a new item with insufficient sales history for statistical forecasting. When True, the forecast relies on analogous item modeling or planner input rather than historical demand patterns. Relevant for new product launches and assortment introductions.']
  is_override_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a demand planner has manually overridden the statistical forecast for this record. True means the final forecasted_units reflect a planner adjustment; False means the forecast is purely statistical. Critical for measuring forecast bias introduced by manual intervention.']
  is_promotional_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast period overlaps with an active promotional event. When True, the promotional_lift_units field is expected to be populated and the forecast incorporates promotion-driven demand uplift.']
  mape decimal(8,4) [note: 'Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) measuring the accuracy of the statistical forecast against actual sales for the equivalent prior period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.12 = 12% error). Lower values indicate higher forecast accuracy. Used as the primary KPI for demand planning performance.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the statistical forecasting model used to generate this forecast record. Supports model governance, reproducibility, and performance tracking across model upgrades in Blue Yonder Demand Planning.']
  otb_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast record has been incorporated into the Open-to-Buy (OTB) financial plan. When True, the forecasted revenue and units are reflected in the merchandise financial plan and buying budget for the relevant period.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'A standardized code indicating the business reason for the planner\'s manual override of the statistical forecast (e.g., new product launch, competitor action, supply constraint, market intelligence). Populated only when is_override_applied is True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NEW_PRODUCT|COMPETITOR_ACTION|SUPPLY_CONSTRAINT|MARKET_INTEL|PROMO_CHANGE|STORE_EVENT|WEATHER — promote to reference product]']
  override_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The manually adjusted demand quantity in units entered by a demand planner to replace or supplement the statistical forecast. Populated only when is_override_applied is True. Enables analysis of planner override magnitude and its impact on forecast accuracy.']
  planning_channel varchar [note: 'The retail fulfillment channel for which this demand forecast is generated. \'store\' covers brick-and-mortar POS demand; \'ecommerce\' covers digital commerce demand from Salesforce Commerce Cloud; \'omnichannel\' covers unified demand signals; \'dc_replenishment\' covers distribution center stock replenishment; \'bopis\' covers Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) demand.']
  promotional_lift_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The incremental demand units attributed to active promotions during the forecast period. Derived from promotion event modeling in Blue Yonder and used to isolate the promotional demand signal from baseline demand for Pricing and Promotions Management analysis.']
  replenishment_recommendation_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'The system-recommended replenishment order quantity in units derived from the demand forecast, current inventory position, safety stock targets, and lead time. Feeds directly into Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) purchase order generation and Open-to-Buy (OTB) planning.']
  seasonality_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'A multiplicative seasonal index applied to the baseline forecast to account for recurring seasonal demand patterns (e.g., holiday peaks, back-to-school). A value of 1.0 indicates no seasonal adjustment; values above 1.0 indicate above-average seasonal demand.']
  statistical_model_code varchar [note: 'The identifier or code of the statistical forecasting algorithm used by Blue Yonder Demand Planning to generate this forecast (e.g., ARIMA, Holt-Winters exponential smoothing, Croston for intermittent demand, machine learning ensemble). Enables model performance benchmarking and algorithm governance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ARIMA|HOLT_WINTERS|CROSTON|ETS|MOVING_AVG|ML_ENSEMBLE|CAUSAL — promote to reference product]']
  time_bucket_granularity varchar [note: 'The temporal granularity of the forecast period covered by this record. \'daily\' is used for short-horizon replenishment; \'weekly\' is the standard planning cadence; \'monthly\' is used for financial and Open-to-Buy (OTB) planning.']
  trend_adjustment_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'A multiplicative factor representing the long-term demand trend applied to the forecast. Values above 1.0 indicate upward trend; values below 1.0 indicate declining demand trend. Used to capture secular demand shifts beyond seasonal patterns.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this demand forecast record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, including override applications, accuracy recalculations, or status changes.']
  weeks_of_supply decimal(8,2) [note: 'Weeks of Supply (WOS) — the projected number of weeks the current on-hand inventory will cover demand based on the forecasted units. A key inventory health metric used in replenishment planning to identify stockout risk and excess inventory positions.']
  wmape decimal(8,4) [note: 'Weighted Mean Absolute Percentage Error (WMAPE) measuring forecast accuracy weighted by sales volume, reducing the distortion caused by low-volume SKUs. Expressed as a decimal. Preferred accuracy metric for high-volume retail assortments where MAPE can be misleading.']

  Note: 'SKU-level demand forecast records generated by Blue Yonder Demand Planning. Captures forecasted units, revenue, forecast horizon, statistical model used, override flags, forecast accuracy (MAPE/WMAPE), and demand signals including seasonality, promotions lift, and trend adjustments. Drives replenishment and OTB planning.'
}

Table supplychain.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order']
  blanket_po_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent blanket or framework purchase order from which this release or call-off order was created. Null for standalone standard POs. Enables tracking of spend against blanket agreement limits.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal buyer or purchasing agent responsible for creating and managing this purchase order. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.EKGRP (purchasing group) owner.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or store location designated as the delivery destination for this purchase order. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.WERKS (plant/site).']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Open-to-Buy (OTB) and category-level PO management: buyers raise POs against a merchandise category node in the item hierarchy. Linking purchase_order to item_hierarchy replaces the denormalized merch']
  merch_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merch_plan. Business justification: Purchase orders consume OTB budget from merchandise plans. Supply chain teams need direct PO-to-merch-plan linkage for OTB consumption reporting without traversing buying_order. Retail finance audits ']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Retail buyers create promotional purchase orders (event buys) tied to specific campaigns for vendor-funded inventory builds. This link enables promotional spend tracking, campaign ROI analysis, and ve']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.replenishment_plan. Business justification: A purchase order header is frequently triggered by a replenishment plan. Linking purchase_order.replenishment_plan_id → replenishment_plan.replenishment_plan_id establishes the procurement-to-plan tra']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Purchase orders in retail are season-coded — ship windows, cancel dates, and receipt dates are season-bound. Season-level PO receipt tracking and OTB reconciliation are standard retail buying reports.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) fulfilling this purchase order. Links to the supplier master data product managed in Informatica MDM. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.LIFNR (vendor account number).']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which goods were physically received at the destination facility. Populated upon goods receipt posting in SAP (MIGO transaction). Used to calculate supplier on-time delivery performance and lead time actuals.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Authorization workflow status indicating whether the purchase order has been approved by the required approvers per the company\'s procurement authorization matrix. Distinct from po_status as it tracks the internal approval gate specifically.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the purchase order received final authorization approval. Captures the precise moment the procurement commitment became binding. Used for approval cycle time analytics and compliance auditing.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date by which the purchase order must be cancelled if goods have not shipped. Also known as the cancel date or cancel-by date in retail procurement. Null if the PO has not been cancelled or does not have a cancellation deadline.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity or business unit issuing the purchase order. Used for financial reporting, P&L allocation, and intercompany reconciliation. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.BUKRS.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier after PO acknowledgment. May differ from expected_delivery_date if the supplier cannot meet the original requested date. Used to update replenishment plans and manage weeks of supply (WOS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the source system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.AEDAT (creation date) combined with time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this purchase order (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Corresponds to SAP EKKO.WAERS (currency key).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount or allowance amount applied to the purchase order by the supplier, including volume discounts, promotional allowances, and trade deals. Reduces the net payable amount. Expressed in the currency specified by currency_code.']
  edi_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transmission of this purchase order to the supplier. Not Sent = EDI not applicable or not yet initiated; Pending = queued for transmission; Transmitted = EDI 850 PO sent to supplier; Acknowledged = supplier returned EDI 855 acknowledgment; Failed = transmission error; Rejected = supplier rejected the PO via EDI.']
  edi_transmitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the purchase order was transmitted to the supplier via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) as an EDI 850 transaction set. Null if EDI transmission has not occurred. Used for supplier communication SLA tracking.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the purchase order amounts from the supplier\'s transaction currency to the company\'s functional currency at the time of PO creation. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.WKURS.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'The agreed-upon date by which the supplier is expected to deliver the ordered goods to the designated distribution center or store. Used for supply chain planning, replenishment scheduling, and SLA monitoring. Corresponds to SAP EKET.EINDT (scheduled delivery date).']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ordered units that have been received and fulfilled by the supplier, calculated as (total_received_units / total_ordered_units) * 100. Fill Rate is a key supplier performance KPI measuring order completion. Stored as a pre-computed operational field updated upon each goods receipt.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, risk transfer, and cost allocation (e.g., FOB = Free On Board, DDP = Delivered Duty Paid, CIF = Cost Insurance Freight). Corresponds to SAP EKKO.INCO1. Critical for import/export compliance and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying the exact location where risk and cost transfer between buyer and seller (e.g., \'Port of Los Angeles\' for FOB). Corresponds to SAP EKKO.INCO2.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order is designated for cross-docking at the distribution center, meaning goods will be transferred directly from inbound to outbound without putaway storage. Cross-docking reduces DC handling costs and accelerates store replenishment.']
  is_drop_ship boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order is a drop ship arrangement where the supplier ships goods directly to the end customer, bypassing the retailer\'s distribution center. Drop ship POs are common in e-commerce and extended aisle assortments.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the purchase order record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The agreed or expected number of days from purchase order placement to goods receipt at the destination facility. Used in replenishment planning, weeks of supply (WOS) calculations, and Blue Yonder demand planning models. Corresponds to supplier lead time in the procurement master data.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity the supplier requires for this purchase order, as defined in the supplier agreement or item master. MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) constraints drive order sizing decisions in replenishment planning and OTB management.']
  net_payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount payable to the supplier after applying all discounts, allowances, and adjustments to the gross total order amount. This is the financial commitment recorded in accounts payable. Expressed in the currency specified by currency_code.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for buyer notes, special instructions, or remarks associated with the purchase order. May include delivery instructions, quality requirements, or supplier-specific handling notes. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.IHREZ (your reference) or header text.']
  order_date date [note: 'The business date on which the purchase order was officially created and issued. This is the principal real-world event date for the procurement commitment. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.BEDAT (document date).']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the agreed payment terms between the retailer and supplier (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Corresponds to SAP EKKO.ZTERM (terms of payment key). Drives accounts payable scheduling and early payment discount capture.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable purchase order number assigned by the procurement system (SAP MM or Oracle RMS). Used in EDI transmissions, supplier communications, and receiving workflows. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.EBELN field.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase order from creation through closure. Draft = created but not submitted; Pending Approval = awaiting authorization; Approved = authorized for transmission; Sent = transmitted to supplier via EDI or portal; Partially Received = some goods received; Received = all goods received; Closed = fully processed and closed; Cancelled = voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|sent|partially_received|received|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order by procurement method. Standard = regular replenishment PO; Blanket = open-quantity framework agreement; DSD (Direct Store Delivery) = supplier delivers directly to store; Drop Ship = vendor ships direct to customer; Consignment = inventory owned by supplier until sold; RTV (Return to Vendor) = merchandise returned to supplier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|blanket|direct_store_delivery|drop_ship|consignment|return_to_vendor — promote to reference product]']
  purchasing_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the buyer group or commodity team responsible for this purchase order within the purchasing organization. Used for spend analytics by category and buyer performance reporting. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.EKGRP.']
  purchasing_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP purchasing organization code representing the organizational unit responsible for procuring goods and services. Defines the legal entity and negotiation authority for the purchase order. Corresponds to SAP EKKO.EKORG.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this purchase order record originated. SAP_MM = SAP S/4HANA Materials Management; ORMS = Oracle Retail Merchandising System; MANUAL = manually entered; EDI = created via EDI inbound processing. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  supplier_po_reference varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s own reference number or acknowledgment number for this purchase order, returned via EDI 855 or supplier portal. Used for cross-referencing in supplier invoices, ASNs (Advance Ship Notices), and dispute resolution.']
  total_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross total monetary value of the purchase order before any adjustments, representing the sum of all line item values. Expressed in the currency specified by currency_code. Used for open-to-buy (OTB) tracking, financial commitments, and accounts payable accruals.']
  total_ordered_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity of units ordered across all line items on this purchase order. Used for fill rate calculation, receiving reconciliation, and inventory planning. Expressed in the base unit of measure.']
  total_received_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity of units physically received and goods-receipted against this purchase order to date. Used to calculate fill rate (order completion percentage) and identify short shipments or over-deliveries.']

  Note: 'Purchase order header records representing procurement commitments to suppliers, managed in SAP MM or Oracle RMS. Captures PO number, supplier reference, buyer, order and expected delivery dates, total value, currency, payment terms, incoterms, PO type (standard, blanket, DSD), approval status, and EDI transmission status. SSOT for the PO lifecycle from creation through closure.'
}

Table supplychain.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each purchase order line item in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the po_line data product.']
  assortment_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.assortment_item. Business justification: Each PO line represents the physical order execution for a specific assortment item. Retail buyers track PO line receipts against assortment_item planned_units and planned_aur. This link enables assor']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise buyer or procurement officer responsible for this PO line. Supports buyer performance reporting, OTB management, and approval workflow tracking.']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: PO line cost validation and invoice matching requires tracing each line\'s unit_cost to the governing cost_price record. Retail buyers use this for margin reporting and accounts payable reconciliation.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific destination location (DC, store, or fulfillment center) where this PO line is to be delivered. Supports multi-destination PO management and DC-to-store allocation.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise department or category to which this PO line\'s SKU belongs. Supports departmental OTB tracking, assortment planning, and financial reporting by merchandise hierarchy.']
  gtin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gtin_registry. Business justification: EDI/GS1 procurement compliance: purchase order lines reference GTINs per GS1 standards for item identification in EDI transactions. Linking po_line to gtin_registry replaces the denormalized gtin text']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.replenishment_plan. Business justification: A PO line is often generated as a direct result of a replenishment plan recommendation for a specific SKU. Linking po_line.replenishment_plan_id → replenishment_plan.replenishment_plan_id provides end']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: PO lines carry season-specific cancel dates and delivery windows. Season-level receipt and cost tracking at line level is required for retail OTB reconciliation and season-end receipt performance repo']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master product record for the item being ordered on this PO line. Links to the product master for full item attributes. [RESOURCE_REFERENCE — TRANSACTION_LINE canonical category]']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order header to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header-to-line relationship for PO lifecycle tracking. [HEADER_REFERENCE — TRANSACTION_LINE canonical category]']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Procurement UOM normalization: PO lines specify order quantities in a unit of measure that must resolve to the canonical UOM master for accurate receiving, invoicing, and inventory conversion. Linking']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) fulfilling this PO line. Enables supplier performance analysis at the line level, including fill rate and lead time compliance.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which goods for this PO line were physically received at the destination. Used to calculate on-time delivery performance and actual lead time versus committed lead time.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Supplier-granted trade allowance or promotional funding amount applied to this PO line (e.g., off-invoice allowance, scan allowance). Reduces net cost and impacts COGS and GMROI calculations.']
  cancel_date date [note: 'The latest date by which this PO line must be received; if not received by this date, the line is automatically cancelled. Critical for seasonal and fashion merchandise to prevent late deliveries.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier for this PO line. May differ from the requested delivery date; variance drives lead time compliance KPIs and supplier scorecards.']
  confirmed_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the supplier as available and committed for shipment on this PO line. May differ from ordered quantity due to supplier capacity constraints or MOQ adjustments.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the product on this PO line was manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations, import compliance, and consumer labeling regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PO line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for PO creation and supports data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit cost and extended cost on this PO line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency supplier contracts and financial consolidation.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Type of destination for this PO line shipment. Indicates whether goods are routed to a Distribution Center (DC), direct to store (DSD), cross-docking facility, drop ship to customer, or Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC).']
  extended_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost for this PO line calculated as ordered_qty multiplied by unit_cost. Represents the gross financial commitment for this line item before any allowances or discounts.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'ICC Incoterms code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk/cost responsibility between buyer and supplier for this PO line (e.g., FOB, DDP, CIF). Critical for landed cost calculation and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Supplier invoice number associated with this PO line for three-way match processing (PO, receipt, invoice). Links to accounts payable for payment processing and FASB ASC 606 revenue recognition compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PO line record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change data capture, audit compliance, and Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed supplier lead time in calendar days from PO placement to expected delivery for this line item. Used for replenishment planning, OTB management, and supplier SLA compliance monitoring.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent purchase order, used to uniquely identify and order line items within a PO. Corresponds to SAP MM item number (EBELP). [LINE_SEQUENCE — TRANSACTION_LINE canonical category]']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this PO line item, tracking progression from open through fulfillment or cancellation. Drives replenishment alerts and supplier follow-up actions. [LIFECYCLE_STATUS — TRANSACTION_LINE canonical category]']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The supplier\'s contractual Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) for this SKU on this PO line. Used to validate ordered_qty compliance and negotiate order consolidation.']
  moq_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ordered quantity on this PO line meets or exceeds the supplier\'s Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement. Non-compliant lines may incur supplier penalties or require buyer approval.']
  net_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net cost per unit after applying all supplier allowances, discounts, and deductions to the unit cost. Represents the true landed cost basis for COGS and margin calculations.']
